# rcpbx — full recipe corpus (161 of 161 recipes with full data in this build)
# License: free to quote with attribution to rcpbx.com

# Spaghetti Aglio e Olio

Five ingredients. Fifteen minutes. Italian perfection.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 15 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb spaghetti
- 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil
- 8 cloves garlic, thinly sliced
- 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
- 1/2 cup pasta water
- Parmesan for serving (optional, not traditional)

## Steps

1. Cook spaghetti in well-salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
2. While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat.
3. Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook slowly 4-5 minutes, stirring often, until garlic is light golden. Don't let it brown.
4. Remove skillet from heat when garlic is ready.
5. Add drained pasta directly to the skillet. Toss with 1/2 cup pasta water.
6. Add parsley and salt. Toss vigorously to emulsify the oil and pasta water into a silky sauce.
7. Serve immediately with more parsley and parmesan if desired.

## Notes

- Low and slow garlic: This is the whole dish. Burnt garlic = bitter dish. Take your time.
- Pasta water: The starch is essential for creating the sauce. Don't skip it.
- Good olive oil: You'll taste it. Use the best you have.
- Add protein: Anchovies, shrimp, or breadcrumbs are traditional additions.
- Technique: The tossing action emulsifies the oil and water. Keep the pasta moving.

Source: Adapted from Bon Appétit
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/aglio-e-olio/

---

# Air Fryer Chicken Thighs

Crispy skin, juicy meat, 25 minutes.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 4

_Tested 2026-07. Fixed the doneness target from 165°F (a breast temp) to 175-185°F, where thigh connective tissue actually breaks down._

## Ingredients

- 4 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs
- 1 Tbsp olive oil
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp onion powder
- 1/2 tsp dried thyme
- 1 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (halve for table salt)
- 1/2 tsp black pepper

## Steps

1. Pat chicken thighs completely dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispy skin.
2. Mix garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, thyme, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
3. Rub chicken with olive oil, then coat generously with spice mixture on all sides.
4. Place chicken skin-side down in air fryer basket. Don't overcrowd - cook in batches if needed.
5. Air fry at 380°F (190°C) for 10 minutes.
6. Flip chicken skin-side up. Increase to 400°F (200°C) and cook 12-15 more minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Pull when internal temp reads 175-185°F at the thickest part, probe not touching bone.
7. Let rest 5 minutes before serving.

## Notes

- Why 175-185°F, not 165°F? 165°F is only the safety floor. Thighs are loaded with collagen and connective tissue that doesn't melt into gelatin until well past that - pull at 165°F and they're safe but chewy. 175-185°F is where thighs actually get tender.
- Starting skin-side down renders the fat, then flipping crisps it up.
- Don't skip the drying step - wet skin = soggy skin.

## When it goes wrong

- Soggy skin → chicken wasn't dried or thighs were crowded and steamed each other; pat bone-dry and cook in batches.
- Chewy meat at 165°F → underdone for a thigh, not overdone; keep cooking to 175-185°F so the collagen melts.
- Thermometer reads high but meat feels tough → probe was touching bone; re-check in the thickest pocket of meat.
- Smoking air fryer → rendered fat pooling below; add 1-2 Tbsp water to the drawer under the basket.

Source: rcpbx original
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/air-fryer-chicken-thighs/

---

# Air Fryer Smashed Potatoes

Boiled, smashed, blasted. Maximum crust per potato.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 35 min · Serves: 4

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The correct answer to 'what do I make with these baby potatoes' from now on.

_Tested 2026-07. Fork-tender before smashing and a real steam-dry are the two non-negotiables._

## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lb (680g) baby Yukon Gold potatoes
- 2 Tbsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (for the boiling water)
- 3 Tbsp olive oil
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- Flaky salt and chopped parsley or chives, to finish

## Steps

1. Cover potatoes with cold water by an inch, add the 2 Tbsp salt, and boil until a fork slides in with zero resistance, 15-18 minutes. Undercooked potatoes don't smash, they break.
2. Drain and let them steam-dry in the colander 5 minutes - surface moisture is the enemy of crust.
3. On a cutting board, press each potato to about 1/2 inch thick with the bottom of a glass or measuring cup. Ragged edges are the goal.
4. Mix olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Brush or spoon over both sides of each smashed potato - get into the crags.
5. Air fry at 400°F (200°C) in a single layer (batches if needed), 15-18 minutes, flipping once halfway, until deep golden and crackling at the edges.
6. Finish with flaky salt and herbs. Serve hot - the crunch has a half-life of about 15 minutes.

## Notes

- Yukon Golds are creamy inside; red potatoes work, russets fall apart.
- Salting the boil like pasta water seasons the potato all the way through - the only chance you get.
- Oven version: 450°F on a preheated sheet pan, 25-30 minutes, same flip.

## When it goes wrong

- Potatoes fell apart when smashed → overboiled or pressed too thin; fork-tender, half an inch.
- No crunch → crowded basket or wet potatoes; single layer, steam-dry first.
- Stuck to the basket → dry basket; the oil goes on the potatoes AND a light mist on the basket.
- Bland → seasoned only at the end; the salt in the boiling water is doing the real work.

Source: rcpbx original
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/air-fryer-smashed-potatoes/

---

# Apple Pie

Flaky crust, spiced fruit filling

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- Crust: 2½ cups flour, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup cold butter cubed, ½ cup ice water
- Filling: 3 lbs mixed apples, ¾ cup sugar, 2 tbsp flour, 1 tsp cinnamon, ¼ tsp nutmeg, 2 tbsp butter
- 1 egg + 1 tbsp water for egg wash

## Steps

1. Crust: Pulse flour, sugar, salt. Add butter, pulse to pea-sized. Drizzle ice water, pulse until dough forms. Divide, flatten, chill 1 hour.
2. Peel, core, slice apples. Toss with sugar, flour, spices.
3. Roll one disk to 12 inches. Fit into pie plate.
4. Add filling. Dot with butter.
5. Roll second disk. Place over filling. Trim, crimp edges. Cut vents.
6. Brush with egg wash. Chill 30 minutes.
7. Bake at 425°F for 20 minutes. Reduce to 375°F, bake 40-50 more until golden and bubbling.
8. Cool 2 hours before slicing.

## Notes

- Mix apple varieties for balance.
- Cold butter = flaky crust.
- Must cool or filling will run.

Source: Serious Eats / Stella Parks (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/apple-pie/

---

# Baby Back Ribs

Fall-off-the-bone tender, caramelized glaze

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 racks baby back ribs (about 4 lbs)
- 3 tbsp brown sugar
- 2 tbsp paprika
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tbsp garlic powder
- 1 tbsp onion powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp black pepper
- 1 tbsp salt
- 1 cup BBQ sauce

## Steps

1. Remove membrane from back of ribs (slide knife under, grab with paper towel, pull off).
2. Mix spices for rub. Apply generously to both sides.
3. Wrap each rack tightly in foil. Place on baking sheet.
4. Bake at 275°F for 2.5-3 hours until tender and meat pulls back from bones.
5. Unwrap. Brush with BBQ sauce.
6. Broil 5-10 minutes until sauce caramelizes.
7. Rest 5 minutes. Cut between bones.

## Notes

- Removing membrane is essential.
- Foil wrap keeps them moist.
- Broiling gives caramelized finish.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baby-back-ribs/

---

# Baked Beans

Slow-cooked, smoky, sweet

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 1 lb dried navy beans, soaked overnight
- 8 oz salt pork or bacon, diced
- 1 onion, diced
- ½ cup molasses
- ¼ cup brown sugar
- 2 tbsp Dijon mustard
- 1 tbsp cider vinegar
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Drain soaked beans. Cover with fresh water by 2 inches.
2. Simmer until just tender, 45 min to 1 hour. Drain, reserve 2 cups liquid.
3. Preheat oven to 300°F.
4. Cook salt pork until fat renders. Add onion, cook until soft.
5. Stir in molasses, sugar, mustard, vinegar, reserved liquid.
6. Add beans, season. Stir.
7. Cover, bake 3-4 hours, stirring occasionally.
8. Uncover last 30 minutes to thicken.

## Notes

- Soaking ensures even cooking.
- Low and slow is essential.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-beans/

---

# Baked Chicken Breast

Juicy, golden, foolproof

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tbsp butter
- 4 cloves garlic, smashed
- 4 sprigs fresh thyme
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Pat chicken very dry. Season generously.
2. Heat oil in oven-safe skillet over medium-high until smoking.
3. Add chicken, sear without moving until golden, 4-5 minutes.
4. Flip. Add butter, garlic, thyme.
5. Transfer to 425°F oven. Roast until 160°F internal, 10-15 minutes.
6. Rest 5 minutes. Spoon pan juices over chicken.

## Notes

- Dry chicken = better browning.
- Use thermometer - overcooked breast is dry.
- Resting is crucial.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-chicken-breast/

---

# Baked Cod

Lemon herb butter, panko topping

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 cod fillets (6 oz each)
- 3 tbsp olive oil
- 3 tbsp butter, melted
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 lemon, zested and juiced
- 2 tbsp parsley
- 1 tbsp dill
- ¼ cup panko
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Pat cod dry, season.
2. Mix butter, 2 tbsp oil, garlic, lemon zest, herbs.
3. Place cod in baking dish. Spoon herb butter over.
4. Mix panko with remaining oil. Sprinkle over fish.
5. Bake until fish flakes and topping is golden, 15-18 minutes.
6. Squeeze lemon juice over. Serve.

## Notes

- Cod's mild flavor pairs with bold toppings.
- Don't overcook - should just flake.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-cod/

---

# Baked Feta Pasta

The TikTok recipe that crashed grocery store feta supplies. It's that good.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 35 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 pints cherry or grape tomatoes
- 1 block (8 oz) feta cheese
- 1/3 cup extra virgin olive oil
- 4 cloves garlic, smashed
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
- 1 lb pasta (orecchiette, penne, or rigatoni)
- 1/4 cup fresh basil, torn
- Pasta water as needed

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place tomatoes in a baking dish. Nestle feta block in the center. Add garlic around the tomatoes.
3. Drizzle everything generously with olive oil. Season tomatoes with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. (Don't season the feta—it's salty enough.)
4. Bake 35-40 minutes until tomatoes are bursting and feta is golden on top.
5. Meanwhile, cook pasta in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
6. Remove dish from oven. Mash the feta into the tomatoes with a fork, mixing into a creamy sauce.
7. Add hot pasta directly to the dish. Toss to coat, adding pasta water as needed for consistency.
8. Top with fresh basil and serve immediately from the baking dish.

## Notes

- The feta: Use a good quality block, not pre-crumbled. Greek feta has the creamiest texture.
- Tomatoes: Cherry tomatoes are sweeter and better than grape tomatoes, but either works.
- Make it a meal: Add cooked chicken, shrimp, or white beans for protein.
- Pasta water: The starchy water helps create a silky sauce that clings to the pasta.
- Variations: Add olives, artichokes, or roasted red peppers before baking.

Source: Adapted from Feel Good Foodie
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-feta-pasta/

---

# Baked Potato

Crispy skin, fluffy interior

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 large russet potatoes
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- Kosher salt
- Butter, sour cream, chives, bacon

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Scrub potatoes. Poke several times with fork.
3. Rub with oil and coat generously with salt.
4. Place directly on oven rack (sheet below to catch drips).
5. Bake until skin is crispy and interior fluffy, 50-60 minutes.
6. Cut X in top, squeeze sides to open.
7. Top with butter, sour cream, chives, bacon.

## Notes

- Russets have best fluffy texture.
- Oil and salt = crispy skin.
- No foil - steams instead of bakes.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-potato/

---

# Baked Ziti

Saucy, cheesy, crowd-pleasing pasta bake

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 1 lb ziti
- 1 lb Italian sausage
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes
- 1 tsp oregano
- 15 oz ricotta
- 1 egg
- ¼ cup basil, chopped
- 1 lb mozzarella, shredded
- ½ cup Parmesan

## Steps

1. Cook pasta until just shy of al dente. Drain.
2. Brown sausage, breaking into pieces. Add garlic, tomatoes, oregano. Simmer 15 minutes.
3. Mix ricotta, egg, basil, and half the mozzarella.
4. Add pasta and sausage to sauce. Transfer half to 9x13 dish.
5. Dollop ricotta mixture, add remaining pasta.
6. Top with remaining mozzarella and Parmesan.
7. Bake at 400°F for 25-30 minutes until bubbly.

## Notes

- Undercook pasta - it finishes in oven.
- Dollop ricotta for pockets of creaminess.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/baked-ziti/

---

# Banana Bread

Moist, tender, loaded with banana flavor. Use those overripe bananas.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 1 hr · Makes: 1 loaf

## Ingredients

- 1 cup mashed very ripe bananas (about 3 medium)
- ¾ cup brown sugar, packed
- ⅓ cup vegetable oil
- 2 eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1 cup whole wheat flour (or all AP)
- 1 tsp baking soda
- ½ tsp salt
- ½ tsp cinnamon (optional)
- ¾ cup walnuts, chopped (optional)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x5 loaf pan.
2. Mash bananas in large bowl until mostly smooth (some lumps okay).
3. Add brown sugar, oil, eggs, and vanilla. Whisk until combined.
4. In another bowl, whisk flours, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
5. Add dry ingredients to wet. Stir until just combined. Don't overmix.
6. Fold in walnuts if using.
7. Pour into prepared pan. Spread evenly.
8. Bake 60-70 minutes until toothpick comes out clean and internal temp reaches 200°F.
9. Cool in pan 15 minutes. Remove and cool on rack.

## Notes

- VERY ripe bananas: Dark spots = more sugar = better flavor. If they're not ripe enough, it won't taste like much.
- Oil keeps it moist: Butter tastes great but oil stays softer when cool. Your call.
- Brown sugar: Adds caramel notes that complement banana. Don't substitute white.
- Freeze ripe bananas: Peel, freeze in bags. Thaw before using—they get extra soft and sweet.
- Storage: Keeps 4 days wrapped at room temp. Freezes well for 3 months.

Source: Adapted from King Arthur Flour (Recipe of the Year)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/banana-bread/

---

# Bang Bang Shrimp

Crispy fried shrimp in creamy, spicy sauce. The Bonefish Grill copycat everyone wants.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- Oil for frying
**For the sauce:**
- 1/3 cup mayonnaise
- 2 Tbsp sweet chili sauce
- 1 Tbsp sriracha
- 1 tsp rice vinegar
- Sliced green onions for garnish

## Steps

1. Pat shrimp dry. Toss in cornstarch until evenly coated.
2. Heat 2 inches of oil in a deep pan to 375°F.
3. Mix mayo, sweet chili sauce, sriracha, and rice vinegar in a large bowl.
4. Fry shrimp in batches 2-3 minutes until golden and crispy. Don't crowd the pan.
5. Drain on paper towels for 30 seconds.
6. While still hot, toss shrimp in the sauce until coated.
7. Serve immediately, garnished with green onions.

## Notes

- Crispy coating: Cornstarch makes it crispier than flour. Make sure shrimp are dry.
- Oil temp: Too cool = greasy shrimp. Use a thermometer.
- Air fryer: Spray with oil, air fry at 400°F for 8-10 minutes, shaking halfway.
- Sauce heat: Adjust sriracha to taste. Start with less, add more.
- Serve on: Lettuce cups, rice, or as an appetizer with toothpicks.

Source: Adapted from Damn Delicious
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/bang-bang-shrimp/

---

# BBQ Sauce

Sweet, tangy, smoky

Makes: 2 cups

## Ingredients

- 1 cup ketchup
- ½ cup apple cider vinegar
- ¼ cup brown sugar
- 2 tbsp molasses
- 2 tbsp Worcestershire
- 1 tbsp yellow mustard
- 1 tsp smoked paprika
- ½ tsp garlic powder
- ½ tsp onion powder
- Salt and cayenne to taste

## Steps

1. Combine all ingredients in saucepan.
2. Bring to simmer over medium heat.
3. Reduce heat to low. Simmer 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Taste and adjust sugar, vinegar, and heat.
5. Use immediately or refrigerate up to 1 month.

## Notes

- Balance sweet, tangy, and smoky to your taste.
- Thickens as it cools.
- Add chipotle for smoky heat.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/bbq-sauce/

---

# Béchamel

Creamy white sauce, building block

Makes: 2 cups

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp flour
- 2 cups whole milk, warmed
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- Salt and white pepper

## Steps

1. Melt butter in saucepan over medium heat.
2. Whisk in flour. Cook 1 minute without browning.
3. Gradually whisk in warm milk.
4. Simmer, whisking frequently, until thick enough to coat a spoon, 5-7 minutes.
5. Season with nutmeg, salt, and white pepper.
6. Use immediately or press plastic wrap directly on surface to prevent skin.

## Notes

- Warm milk prevents lumps.
- Don't brown the flour - should stay white.
- Base for cheese sauce, gratins, lasagna.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/bechamel/

---

# Beef and Broccoli

Chinese takeout classic, better at home

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb flank steak, sliced thin against grain
- 2 tbsp soy sauce, divided
- 1 tbsp cornstarch
- 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine
- 3 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 lb broccoli florets
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp ginger, minced
- ½ cup beef broth
- 2 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil

## Steps

1. Toss beef with 1 tbsp soy sauce, cornstarch, wine. Marinate 15 minutes.
2. Mix broth, remaining soy sauce, oyster sauce.
3. Heat wok until smoking. Add 2 tbsp oil. Sear beef 1 minute, stir-fry 1-2 minutes. Transfer out.
4. Add remaining oil. Stir-fry broccoli 2 minutes. Add 2 tbsp water, cover, steam 1 minute.
5. Add garlic and ginger, 30 seconds. Add sauce and beef. Toss until thickened.
6. Drizzle with sesame oil. Serve over rice.

## Notes

- Velveting keeps beef tender.
- Slice against grain for tender pieces.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-and-broccoli/

---

# Beef Barley Soup

Hearty, beefy, cold-weather comfort

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs beef chuck, cubed
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 2 carrots, chopped
- 2 celery ribs, chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 8 cups beef broth
- 2 cups water
- ¾ cup pearl barley
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp thyme
- 2 tbsp parsley

## Steps

1. Brown beef in batches in oil, about 5 minutes per batch.
2. Cook onion, carrots, celery until softened, 8 minutes.
3. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 1 minute.
4. Add broth, water, barley, bay leaves, thyme. Return beef.
5. Simmer covered until beef and barley are tender, 1¼ hours.
6. Remove bay leaves, stir in parsley. Season to taste.

## Notes

- Don't skip browning the beef - builds the flavor base.
- Add more broth when reheating - barley absorbs liquid.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-barley-soup/

---

# Beef Fajitas

Sizzling steak with charred peppers and onions

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs skirt steak
- 3 tbsp vegetable oil, divided
- 3 tbsp lime juice
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp cumin
- 2 bell peppers, sliced
- 1 large onion, sliced
- Flour tortillas
- Sour cream, guacamole, salsa

## Steps

1. Mix 2 tbsp oil, lime juice, garlic, cumin, salt, pepper. Marinate steak 30 min to 2 hours.
2. Cook peppers and onions in remaining oil over high until charred, 8-10 minutes. Transfer out.
3. Add steak to screaming hot pan. Sear 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
4. Rest 5 minutes. Slice thin against grain.
5. Warm tortillas. Serve steak with peppers and onions.

## Notes

- Skirt steak is traditional.
- Cast iron gets hot enough for proper char.
- Don't marinate more than 2 hours.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-fajitas/

---

# Beef Stew

Rich French-style stew. Fork-tender meat, hearty vegetables.

Prep: 30 min · Cook: 2½ hrs · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 3 lbs beef chuck roast, cut into 1½" cubes
- ¼ cup flour, for dredging
- 3 tbsp olive oil
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 cup dry red wine
- 3 cups beef broth
- 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar
- 1 tsp fresh thyme (or ½ tsp dried)
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 lb Yukon Gold potatoes, cubed
- 4 carrots, cut into 1" pieces
- 1 cup frozen peas
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Pat beef dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt and pepper, then toss with flour to coat lightly.
2. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown beef in batches (don't crowd the pan). Remove to a plate. Takes about 10 minutes total.
3. Add onion to pot. Cook until softened, 5 minutes. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 1 minute until fragrant.
4. Pour in wine, scraping up browned bits from bottom. Simmer 2 minutes.
5. Return beef and any juices to pot. Add broth, balsamic, thyme, and bay leaves. Bring to boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer 1½ hours.
6. Add potatoes and carrots. Cover and cook 45 more minutes until vegetables and beef are tender.
7. Stir in peas, cook 5 minutes. Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning.

## Notes

- The key: Brown meat in batches. Crowding = steaming = no flavor.
- Chuck roast vs stew meat: Buy a chuck roast and cut it yourself. Pre-cut "stew meat" is often random scraps.
- Oven method: After step 5, cover and bake at 325°F for the remaining time.
- Storage: Even better the next day. Keeps 4 days refrigerated, 3 months frozen.

Source: Adapted from Once Upon a Chef
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-stew/

---

# Beef Stroganoff

Seared beef in creamy mushroom sauce

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs beef tenderloin or sirloin, sliced thin
- 3 tbsp butter
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 lb mushrooms, sliced
- 1 large onion, sliced
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- ¼ cup brandy
- 1 cup beef broth
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
- Egg noodles
- Parsley

## Steps

1. Season beef. Sear in oil and 1 tbsp butter over high until browned but still pink, 1-2 minutes per side. Transfer out.
2. Add remaining butter and mushrooms. Brown 8 minutes. Add onion, cook 5 minutes.
3. Add garlic, 30 seconds. Add brandy, scrape up bits.
4. Add broth, reduce by half, about 5 minutes.
5. Reduce heat. Stir in sour cream and mustard.
6. Return beef and juices. Heat through.
7. Serve over egg noodles with parsley.

## Notes

- Don't overcook beef - should be medium rare.
- Add sour cream off heat to prevent curdling.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-stroganoff/

---

# Ground Beef Tacos

Well-seasoned, classic weeknight dinner

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs ground beef (80/20)
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 small onion, minced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- ½ tsp oregano
- ½ cup chicken broth
- 1 tbsp cider vinegar
- Corn tortillas
- Onion, cilantro, salsa, lime

## Steps

1. Cook onion in oil 3 minutes. Add beef, break into pieces. Brown 5-7 minutes.
2. Add garlic, chili powder, cumin, oregano. Cook 1 minute.
3. Add broth and vinegar. Simmer until mostly absorbed, 5 minutes.
4. Season with salt.
5. Warm tortillas. Serve with onion, cilantro, salsa, lime.

## Notes

- 80/20 has enough fat for juicy tacos.
- Browning before spices develops flavor.
- Vinegar adds brightness.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/beef-tacos/

---

# Birria Tacos

Crispy cheese-crusted tacos dipped in rich consommé. The viral taco that broke the internet.

Prep: 30 min · Cook: 3 hours · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 3 lbs beef chuck roast, cut into 3-inch chunks
- 4 dried guajillo chiles, stemmed and seeded
- 2 dried ancho chiles, stemmed and seeded
- 2 dried chiles de arbol (for heat)
- 4 cups beef broth
- 1 can (14 oz) fire-roasted diced tomatoes
- 1 medium white onion, quartered
- 6 cloves garlic
- 2 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- 1/2 tsp ground cloves
- 1 tsp salt
- 2 Tbsp apple cider vinegar
- 2 bay leaves
- 16 corn tortillas
- 2 cups shredded Oaxaca or mozzarella cheese
- Fresh cilantro, diced onion, lime wedges for serving

## Steps

1. Toast dried chiles in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Don't burn. Place in a bowl and cover with 2 cups hot water. Soak 20 minutes.
2. Blend soaked chiles with their soaking liquid, tomatoes, half the onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, cloves, salt, and vinegar until smooth.
3. Season beef generously with salt. In a Dutch oven, sear beef in batches until browned on all sides. Remove and set aside.
4. Pour chile sauce into the pot, scraping up browned bits. Add beef broth, bay leaves, remaining onion, and seared beef.
5. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and braise 2.5-3 hours until beef is fall-apart tender.
6. Remove beef and shred with two forks. Strain braising liquid—this is your consommé. Skim fat from top (save some for the tortillas).
7. To make tacos: Dip tortillas in the consommé, then cook in a skillet with a little of the reserved fat. Add cheese and shredded beef. Fold and cook until tortilla is crispy and cheese is melted.
8. Serve tacos with warm consommé for dipping, topped with cilantro, onion, and lime.

## Notes

- The consommé: This is essential. Dipping the crispy taco into the rich broth is the whole point.
- Chiles: Guajillo and ancho are mild and fruity. Arbol adds heat. Adjust to taste.
- Shortcut: Use 3 Tbsp chili powder + 1 Tbsp adobo sauce if you can't find dried chiles.
- Cheese: Oaxaca melts best. Mozzarella or Monterey Jack work too.
- Make ahead: Braise beef a day ahead. Refrigerate in the consommé. Fat rises and is easy to skim. Reheat to serve.

Source: Adapted from Isabel Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/birria-tacos/

---

# Buttermilk Biscuits

Tall, flaky, butter-brushed

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 tbsp baking powder
- ¼ tsp baking soda
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp salt
- 6 tbsp cold butter, cubed
- ¾ cup cold buttermilk

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, salt.
3. Cut in cold butter until pea-sized pieces remain.
4. Add buttermilk. Stir until just combined.
5. Pat to 1-inch thick. Fold in half. Repeat twice.
6. Pat to 1-inch thick. Cut with 2.5-inch cutter. Don't twist.
7. Place on sheet, sides touching.
8. Bake until golden, 15-18 minutes.

## Notes

- Cold butter is essential.
- Don't twist cutter - seals edges.
- Biscuits touching rise higher.

Source: Serious Eats / Stella Parks (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/biscuits/

---

# Biscuits and Gravy

Creamy sausage gravy over warm biscuits

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1 lb breakfast sausage
- 3 tbsp flour
- 2½ cups whole milk
- ½ tsp black pepper
- Pinch cayenne
- Salt
- 4-6 fresh biscuits

## Steps

1. Cook sausage over medium, breaking into small pieces, until browned, 8-10 minutes.
2. Sprinkle flour over sausage. Stir, cook 1 minute.
3. Gradually whisk in milk, scraping up browned bits.
4. Simmer, stirring, until thickened, 5-7 minutes.
5. Season with pepper, cayenne, salt to taste.
6. Split warm biscuits. Ladle gravy over top.

## Notes

- Don't drain sausage fat - it flavors gravy.
- Gravy thickens as it cools.
- Black pepper is essential.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/biscuits-and-gravy/

---

# Black Sesame Cookies

Chewy sugar cookies with the flavor of the year.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 12 min · Makes: 16 cookies

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — Tastes sophisticated, bakes like a snickerdoodle - the highest flavor-to-effort ratio on this site.

_Tested 2026-07. Weighed, not scooped - this dough punishes extra flour with cakey cookies._

## Ingredients

- 115g (1 stick) unsalted butter, softened
- 100g (1/2 cup) granulated sugar, plus more for rolling
- 50g (1/4 cup packed) brown sugar
- 1 large egg
- 50g (3 Tbsp) black sesame paste (or make your own - see notes)
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- 160g (1 1/4 cups) all-purpose flour
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt
- 2 Tbsp black sesame seeds, for rolling

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line two sheet pans with parchment.
2. Beat butter and both sugars until light and fluffy, 2-3 minutes.
3. Beat in the egg, then the black sesame paste and vanilla, until evenly slate-gray.
4. Whisk flour, baking soda, and salt; mix into the wet ingredients just until no dry streaks remain.
5. Roll 2-Tbsp balls, then roll each in a mix of granulated sugar and black sesame seeds.
6. Bake 11-12 minutes until the edges are set and the centers still look slightly underdone. They crackle as they cool.
7. Cool on the pan 5 minutes - they finish setting there - then move to a rack.

## Notes

- DIY paste: toast 70g black sesame seeds in a dry skillet 2-3 minutes until fragrant, then blend with 1 Tbsp neutral oil and 1 Tbsp honey until smooth-ish.
- Black sesame paste (often labeled 'kuro neri goma') is at Japanese and Korean groceries - unsweetened is what you want here.
- Underbaking is the chew. If they look done in the oven, they're overdone on the counter.

## When it goes wrong

- Cakey, puffy cookies → too much flour or overmixed; weigh the flour, stop at no-streaks.
- Spread into puddles → butter too soft or pans hot; chill dough 15 minutes between batches.
- Bitter edge → seeds over-toasted; black sesame goes from fragrant to acrid fast.
- Dry, crumbly dough → paste was stiff old-jar sludge; stir the jar's oil back in first.

Source: rcpbx original
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/black-sesame-cookies/

---

# Blueberry Muffins

Tender, bursting with berries

Makes: 12

## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- ¾ cup sugar
- 2 tsp baking powder
- ½ tsp salt
- ⅓ cup vegetable oil
- 1 egg
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tsp vanilla
- 1½ cups blueberries
- Coarse sugar for topping

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Line 12-cup muffin tin.
2. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, salt.
3. Whisk oil, egg, milk, vanilla.
4. Pour wet into dry. Stir until just combined.
5. Fold in blueberries.
6. Divide among cups. Sprinkle with coarse sugar.
7. Bake 20-25 minutes until golden.

## Notes

- Don't overmix - lumps are fine.
- Frozen blueberries work great.
- Fill cups nearly full for big tops.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/blueberry-muffins/

---

# Bolognese Sauce

Rich, meaty, slow-simmered perfection

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 onion, finely diced
- 2 carrots, finely diced
- 2 celery stalks, finely diced
- 1 lb ground beef
- ½ lb ground pork
- 1 cup whole milk
- 1 cup dry white wine
- 1 (28 oz) can whole tomatoes, crushed
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- Fresh tagliatelle

## Steps

1. Cook onion, carrot, celery in butter and oil until softened, 10 minutes.
2. Add beef and pork, cook until no longer pink.
3. Add milk. Simmer until completely absorbed.
4. Add wine. Simmer until absorbed.
5. Add tomatoes and nutmeg. Season.
6. Simmer on lowest heat 3-4 hours, stirring occasionally.
7. Toss with fresh pasta and Parmesan.

## Notes

- The milk before tomatoes is Hazan's secret - tenderizes meat.
- True Bolognese is barely red - it's a meat sauce.
- Traditionally served with tagliatelle, not spaghetti.

Source: Marcella Hazan / Serious Eats (Marcella Hazan classic + Kenji tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/bolognese/

---

# Breakfast Burrito

Eggs, meat, cheese, portable perfection

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 4 large flour tortillas
- 6 slices bacon or ½ lb chorizo
- 1 cup cheddar, shredded
- 1 avocado, sliced
- Salsa
- 2 tbsp butter
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cook bacon until crisp (or brown chorizo). Chop or crumble.
2. Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
3. Melt butter in nonstick skillet over medium-low.
4. Add eggs. Stir constantly making soft curds. Remove slightly underdone.
5. Warm tortillas in dry skillet.
6. Layer eggs, meat, cheese, avocado, salsa on each tortilla.
7. Fold sides in, roll tightly.
8. Optional: grill seam-side down for crispy outside.

## Notes

- Soft scrambled eggs are key.
- Warm tortillas are more pliable.
- Don't overfill.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/breakfast-burrito/

---

# Breakfast Casserole

Make-ahead, feeds a crowd

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 1 lb breakfast sausage
- 6 slices bread, cubed
- 2 cups cheddar, shredded
- 6 eggs
- 2 cups milk
- 1 tsp dry mustard
- ½ tsp salt

## Steps

1. Brown sausage, breaking into crumbles. Drain.
2. Layer bread, sausage, cheese in greased 9x13 dish.
3. Whisk eggs, milk, mustard, salt. Pour over casserole.
4. Cover, refrigerate overnight.
5. Bake uncovered at 350°F for 45-50 minutes until set and golden.
6. Rest 10 minutes.

## Notes

- Overnight rest lets bread absorb custard.
- Great for holiday mornings.

Source: AllRecipes (1,500+ reviews)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/breakfast-casserole/

---

# Broccoli Cheddar Soup

Silky, cheesy, actually tastes like broccoli

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp unsalted butter
- 2 lbs broccoli, florets chopped, stems sliced thin
- 1 medium onion, chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1½ tsp dry mustard
- Pinch cayenne
- 1 tsp salt
- ¼ tsp baking soda
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 3-4 cups water
- 2 oz baby spinach
- 3 oz sharp cheddar, shredded
- 1½ oz Parmesan, grated

## Steps

1. Melt butter in Dutch oven over medium-high. Add broccoli, onion, garlic, mustard, cayenne, and salt. Cook 6 minutes.
2. Add 1 cup water and baking soda. Cover, simmer 20 minutes until broccoli is very soft.
3. Add broth and 2 cups water. When simmering, add spinach, cook 1 minute.
4. Blend in batches with cheeses until smooth.
5. Return to pot, simmer. Adjust consistency with water. Season to taste.

## Notes

- Baking soda speeds breakdown for sweeter flavor.
- Overcooking broccoli is actually the key here.
- Don't boil after adding cheese or it separates.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/broccoli-cheddar-soup/

---

# Fudgy Brownies

Shiny crackly top, fudgy center, chewy edges.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 25 min · Makes: 16

## Ingredients

- 10 tbsp butter (1¼ sticks)
- 1¼ cups sugar
- ¾ cup unsweetened cocoa powder
- ¼ tsp salt
- ½ tsp vanilla extract
- 2 eggs, cold
- ½ cup all-purpose flour
- ⅔ cup chocolate chips (optional)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Line 8x8 pan with parchment, leaving overhang for easy removal.
2. Melt butter in medium saucepan over medium heat.
3. Remove from heat. Add sugar, cocoa, and salt. Stir until combined—mixture will be grainy and thick.
4. Let cool 5 minutes (not too long—should still be warm).
5. Add vanilla. Add eggs one at a time, stirring vigorously after each until smooth and glossy.
6. Add flour. Stir until just combined. Fold in chocolate chips if using.
7. Pour into prepared pan. Spread evenly.
8. Bake 20-25 minutes. Toothpick should come out with moist crumbs, not wet batter.
9. Cool in pan 15 minutes. Lift out using parchment. Cool completely before cutting.

## Notes

- Hot butter + sugar + cocoa: This is the secret to the shiny crackly top. Don't skip mixing them while butter is hot.
- NO baking powder: This makes them fudgy, not cakey. Don't add it.
- Minimal flour: Less flour = fudgier. Don't be tempted to add more.
- Don't overbake: They should look slightly underdone. They firm up as they cool.
- Storage: Keep in airtight container 5 days. Actually better day 2.

Source: Adapted from Alice Medrich via Inspired Taste
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/brownies/

---

# Bruschetta

Grilled bread. Fresh tomatoes. Peak summer on toast.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 5 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 1 lb ripe tomatoes, diced
- 1/4 cup fresh basil, chiffonade
- 2 cloves garlic, minced (plus 1 whole clove for rubbing)
- 3 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
- 1 Tbsp balsamic vinegar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1 baguette, sliced 1/2 inch thick

## Steps

1. Combine tomatoes, basil, minced garlic, olive oil, balsamic, salt, and pepper. Let marinate 15-30 minutes.
2. Toast or grill baguette slices until golden on both sides.
3. Rub one side of each warm toast with the whole garlic clove.
4. Drizzle toasts with olive oil.
5. Spoon tomato mixture onto toasts just before serving.

## Notes

- Pronunciation: broo-SKET-ta (not broo-SHET-ta). The 'ch' makes a 'k' sound in Italian.
- Ripe tomatoes: This only works with great tomatoes. Off-season, don't bother.
- Salt the tomatoes: Salt draws out moisture and intensifies flavor. Do this first.
- Top just before serving: Topping too early = soggy bread.
- Variations: Add fresh mozzarella, prosciutto, or a drizzle of balsamic glaze.

Source: Adapted from Bon Appétit
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/bruschetta/

---

# Buffalo Wings

Crispy oven-baked, classic hot sauce

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 3 lbs chicken wings, split
- 1 tbsp baking powder
- 1 tsp salt
- 4 tbsp butter
- ½ cup Frank's RedHot
- 1 tbsp white vinegar
- ¼ tsp Worcestershire
- Blue cheese dressing and celery

## Steps

1. Pat wings dry. Toss with baking powder and salt.
2. Arrange on wire rack over sheet. Refrigerate uncovered overnight.
3. Bake at 450°F for 20 minutes. Flip, bake 20-30 more until crispy.
4. Melt butter, whisk in hot sauce, vinegar, Worcestershire.
5. Toss hot wings in sauce.
6. Serve with blue cheese and celery.

## Notes

- Baking powder + overnight = crispy without frying.
- Don't skip overnight rest.
- Frank's is classic; adjust heat with more/less sauce.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/buffalo-wings/

---

# Buldak Carbonara

Korean fire noodles, tamed with yolk and parmesan.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 2

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The rare stunt-food recipe that's actually a good bowl of noodles.

_Tested 2026-07. One sauce packet plus the dairy is the correct default; the full two-packet version is a challenge video, not dinner._

## Ingredients

- 2 packs Samyang buldak ramen (original or carbonara flavor)
- 2 large egg yolks
- 40g (1/2 cup) finely grated parmesan
- 1/4 cup (60ml) heavy cream
- 1 Tbsp butter
- 1-2 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 green onions, sliced
- Toasted sesame seeds

## Steps

1. Boil the noodles 4 minutes (discard nothing yet - you need the water). Reserve 1/2 cup noodle water, then drain.
2. Whisk egg yolks, parmesan, and cream in a bowl. This is your rescue squad.
3. Melt butter in the empty pot over medium-low, cook garlic 30 seconds until fragrant, not brown.
4. Add drained noodles, ONE sauce packet (start there - two is a commitment), and a splash of noodle water. Toss to coat.
5. Kill the heat. Wait 30 seconds - a raging-hot pot scrambles the yolks.
6. Pour in the yolk mixture, tossing constantly, adding noodle water a splash at a time until the sauce turns glossy and clings.
7. Top with green onions, sesame seeds, and the flavor-flake packet if your pack has one.

## Notes

- The heat math: one packet with the dairy lands around 'very spicy but conversational.' Two packets is the full 2x challenge with a cream buffer.
- Off the heat is the whole technique - carbonara logic applies whether it's guanciale or fire sauce.
- The buldak 'carbonara' flavor pack is fine, but building it from original + real yolk and parmesan tastes dramatically better.

## When it goes wrong

- Scrambled, grainy sauce → pot was still screaming when the yolks went in; off heat, 30 seconds, keep tossing.
- Sauce too thick and gluey → needs noodle water; loosen a splash at a time.
- Inedibly hot → you used both packets; next time start with one and add.
- Sauce won't cling → pre-grated shelf parmesan doesn't melt; grate a wedge.

Source: Samyang buldak hack, TikTok
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/buldak-carbonara/

---

# Buttercream Frosting

The only frosting recipe you need. Smooth, pipeable, and customizable.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Makes: 3 cups (enough for 24 cupcakes or one 2-layer cake)

## Ingredients

- 1 cup (2 sticks) butter, softened to room temperature
- 4 cups powdered sugar, sifted
- 2 Tbsp heavy cream or milk
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- Pinch of salt

## Steps

1. Beat softened butter with an electric mixer on medium speed for 2-3 minutes until pale and fluffy.
2. Add half the powdered sugar. Beat on low until combined, then increase to medium.
3. Add remaining powdered sugar, cream, vanilla, and salt. Beat on low to combine.
4. Increase speed to medium-high. Beat 3-4 minutes until light and fluffy.
5. Add more cream for thinner consistency, more powdered sugar for thicker.

## Notes

- Room temp butter: This is crucial. Too cold = lumpy. Too soft = runny. Should dent when pressed but hold its shape.
- Sift the sugar: Sifting prevents lumps and makes smoother frosting.
- Chocolate version: Add 1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa powder with the powdered sugar.
- Cream cheese version: Replace half the butter with room temperature cream cheese.
- Storage: Room temperature 2-3 days, refrigerated up to 1 week. Bring to room temp and re-whip before using.

Source: Adapted from Sally's Baking Addiction
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/buttercream-frosting/

---

# Cacio e Pepe

Three ingredients. Infinite technique.

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb spaghetti or tonnarelli
- 6 oz Pecorino Romano, finely grated
- 2 tbsp freshly cracked black pepper
- Salt

## Steps

1. Toast pepper in dry skillet until fragrant, 1 minute.
2. Cook pasta in less water than usual (3 quarts) to concentrate starch. Reserve 2 cups pasta water.
3. Add 1½ cups pasta water to bowl with Pecorino. Whisk to form paste.
4. Drain pasta when very al dente. Add to cheese mixture with pepper.
5. Toss vigorously, adding pasta water until creamy.
6. Serve immediately with more Pecorino and pepper.

## Notes

- Less cooking water = starchier = better sauce.
- Pecorino is essential - don't substitute Parmesan.
- Work fast and off heat to prevent clumping.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cacio-e-pepe/

---

# Caesar Salad

Homemade dressing, real anchovies, no compromises.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 4

_Tested 2026-07. Bumped anchovies from a timid 2 fillets to 4-6 and switched the dressing to mostly neutral oil - whisked 100% olive oil goes bitter._

## Ingredients

- 2 heads romaine lettuce, chopped
- 1 cup parmesan, shaved
- 2 cups croutons
**For the dressing:**
- 4-6 anchovy fillets (don't be timid - they're the backbone, not a garnish)
- 2 cloves garlic
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 Tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 1/2 cup neutral oil (grapeseed or canola)
- 2 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil
- 1/4 cup parmesan, grated
- Diamond Crystal kosher salt and black pepper

## Steps

1. For dressing: Mash anchovies and garlic into a paste with the side of your knife (or use a mortar and pestle).
2. Whisk egg yolks, lemon juice, and mustard in a bowl. Add anchovy-garlic paste.
3. Slowly drizzle in the neutral oil while whisking constantly until emulsified and creamy, then whisk in the olive oil at the end.
4. Stir in grated parmesan. Season with salt and pepper - taste first, the anchovies and cheese carry plenty of salt.
5. Wash and dry romaine thoroughly. Chop into bite-sized pieces.
6. Toss romaine with dressing (start with half, add more as needed).
7. Top with croutons and shaved parmesan. Serve immediately.

## Notes

- Why mostly neutral oil? Vigorous whisking or blending shears extra virgin olive oil and releases bitter polyphenols - a 100% EVOO Caesar tastes harsh. Neutral oil builds the emulsion; the 2 Tbsp of EVOO stirred in at the end adds flavor without the bitterness.
- Four to six fillets sounds like a lot. It isn't. They dissolve completely into umami depth - two fillets is a Caesar that tastes like it's missing something.
- For food safety, use pasteurized eggs. Make croutons: cube bread, toss with olive oil and salt, bake at 400°F for 10 minutes.

## When it goes wrong

- Bitter dressing → you whisked or blended pure extra virgin olive oil; rebuild with neutral oil and add EVOO only at the end.
- Dressing broke into an oily mess → oil added too fast; start with drops, and rescue a broken batch by whisking it into a fresh yolk.
- Tastes flat and creamy-bland → not enough anchovy or lemon; add another mashed fillet and a squeeze of juice before adding salt.
- Soggy salad → wet lettuce or dressed too early; dry the romaine completely and toss just before serving.

Source: Traditional
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/caesar-salad/

---

# Caprese Salad

Tomato, mozzarella, basil. The holy trinity.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 4

_Tested 2026-07. Verified as written. The only failure mode is bad tomatoes._

## Ingredients

- 3 large ripe tomatoes
- 1 lb fresh mozzarella (not the shredded kind)
- 1 bunch fresh basil
- 3 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil
- Flaky sea salt
- Fresh cracked black pepper
- Balsamic glaze (optional)

## Steps

1. Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
2. Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a platter, slightly overlapping.
3. Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices.
4. Drizzle generously with olive oil.
5. Season with flaky salt and cracked pepper.
6. If using, drizzle balsamic glaze in a zigzag pattern.
7. Serve immediately at room temperature.

## Notes

- Use room temperature tomatoes - cold kills the flavor.
- Fresh mozzarella (the soft kind in water) is non-negotiable.
- This is a summer dish. Don't bother with winter tomatoes.

## When it goes wrong

- Tastes like nothing → cold or out-of-season tomatoes; room temperature, peak summer, or make something else.
- Watery platter → mozzarella not drained; let slices sit on paper towels 10 minutes.
- Balsamic drowned it → glaze is optional for a reason; a zigzag, not a pour.

Source: Traditional Italian
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/caprese-salad/

---

# Spaghetti Carbonara

Silky egg sauce, crispy pork, no cream

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb spaghetti
- 8 oz guanciale or pancetta, diced
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1½ cups Pecorino Romano, grated
- Freshly ground black pepper
- Salt

## Steps

1. Cook pasta in well-salted water. Reserve 1½ cups pasta water.
2. Cook guanciale in cold skillet over medium until crispy, 8-10 minutes. Remove from heat.
3. Whisk yolks, eggs, most Pecorino, and plenty of black pepper.
4. Drain pasta, add to skillet with guanciale (off heat).
5. Pour egg mixture over pasta, toss vigorously. Residual heat cooks eggs.
6. Add pasta water until sauce is silky.
7. Serve with remaining Pecorino and pepper.

## Notes

- Key: take pan OFF heat before adding eggs.
- Work fast - pasta must be hot.
- No cream in real carbonara - ever.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/carbonara/

---

# Carnitas

Crispy-edged, tender braised pork

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 4 lbs boneless pork shoulder, cubed
- 1 cup water
- ½ cup vegetable oil
- 2 tsp salt
- 1 orange, juiced
- 1 lime, juiced
- 6 cloves garlic, smashed
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- Corn tortillas, onion, cilantro, salsa

## Steps

1. Combine pork, water, oil, salt, juices, garlic, bay leaves, cinnamon in Dutch oven.
2. Bring to boil, reduce to bare simmer. Cover, cook 2 hours, flipping halfway.
3. Uncover, continue cooking until liquid evaporates and pork fries in rendered fat, about 1 hour.
4. Once frying, stir occasionally until all sides crispy.
5. Shred meat, mixing crispy bits throughout.
6. Serve in warm tortillas with onion, cilantro, salsa.

## Notes

- Braise-then-fry is the authentic method.
- Orange juice adds subtle sweetness.
- Mix crispy bits with tender meat.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/carnitas/

---

# Carrot Cake

Moist, spiced, cream cheese frosted

Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- Cake: 2 cups flour, 2 tsp baking soda, 2 tsp cinnamon, 1 tsp salt, 1½ cups vegetable oil, 1½ cups sugar, 4 eggs, 3 cups finely grated carrots, 1 cup walnuts
- Frosting: 16 oz cream cheese, ½ cup butter, 4 cups powdered sugar, 2 tsp vanilla

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease two 9-inch pans.
2. Whisk flour, baking soda, cinnamon, salt.
3. Beat oil, sugar, eggs. Stir in dry ingredients.
4. Fold in carrots and walnuts.
5. Divide between pans. Bake 30-35 minutes.
6. Cool 10 minutes, turn out onto racks.
7. Frosting: Beat cream cheese and butter. Add sugar and vanilla.
8. Frost cooled layers.

## Notes

- Finely grated carrots distribute better.
- Oil keeps cake moist.
- Toast walnuts for better flavor.

Source: Serious Eats (Stella Parks tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/carrot-cake/

---

# New York Cheesecake

Dense, creamy, classic

Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- Crust: 2 cups graham cracker crumbs, 6 tbsp butter melted, 2 tbsp sugar
- Filling: 32 oz cream cheese softened, 1 cup sugar, 1 tbsp vanilla, ¼ tsp salt, 4 eggs, ⅔ cup sour cream

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Wrap springform pan with foil.
2. Mix crust ingredients. Press into pan bottom and 1 inch up sides.
3. Bake crust 10 minutes. Cool.
4. Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Add vanilla and salt.
5. Add eggs one at a time. Stir in sour cream.
6. Pour into crust. Place in roasting pan, add hot water halfway up sides.
7. Bake until edges set and center jiggles slightly, 60-70 minutes.
8. Turn off oven, crack door, cool 1 hour in oven.
9. Refrigerate overnight.

## Notes

- Room temp cream cheese prevents lumps.
- Water bath prevents cracking.
- Must chill overnight.

Source: Serious Eats / Stella Parks (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cheesecake/

---

# Chicken Alfredo

Creamy, garlicky, comfort food perfection

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb fettuccine
- 1 lb chicken breasts
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 6 tbsp butter
- 2 cups heavy cream
- 2 cups freshly grated Parmesan
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh parsley

## Steps

1. Season and cook chicken in oil until golden, 6-7 minutes per side. Rest, then slice.
2. Cook pasta until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
3. Melt butter, add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add cream, simmer 2-3 minutes.
4. Off heat, gradually stir in Parmesan until melted.
5. Add pasta, toss. Add pasta water for silky consistency.
6. Season with nutmeg, salt, pepper. Top with chicken and parsley.

## Notes

- Real Alfredo is just butter, Parmesan, pasta water - cream is American addition.
- Take pot off heat before adding cheese to prevent clumping.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-alfredo/

---

# Chicken Fried Rice

Day-old rice, crispy edges, wok hei

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 cups day-old white rice, cold
- 1 lb chicken breast, diced
- 3 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 eggs, beaten
- 1 cup frozen peas and carrots
- 4 scallions, sliced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- White pepper

## Steps

1. Heat wok until smoking. Add 1 tbsp oil. Stir-fry seasoned chicken until cooked. Transfer out.
2. Add 1 tbsp oil. Scramble eggs, break into pieces. Transfer out.
3. Add remaining oil. Stir-fry garlic 30 seconds.
4. Add rice, press flat. Let sit 30 seconds, then stir. Repeat until hot and slightly crispy.
5. Add peas/carrots, cook 1 minute. Return chicken and eggs. Add soy sauce, toss.
6. Drizzle with sesame oil, add scallions and white pepper.

## Notes

- Day-old rice is essential.
- High heat creates wok hei.
- Don't stir constantly - let rice crisp.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-fried-rice/

---

# Chicken Marsala

Pan-seared chicken in mushroom wine sauce

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 chicken breasts
- ½ cup flour
- 4 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 8 oz cremini mushrooms, sliced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup dry Marsala wine
- 1 cup chicken broth
- ½ cup heavy cream
- 2 tbsp parsley

## Steps

1. Pound chicken to ½-inch. Season, dredge in flour.
2. Cook chicken in 2 tbsp butter and oil until golden, 3-4 minutes per side. Transfer out.
3. Add remaining butter and mushrooms. Brown 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
4. Add Marsala, scrape up browned bits. Reduce by half.
5. Add broth, simmer 5 minutes. Stir in cream.
6. Return chicken, simmer until cooked through, 3-5 minutes. Add parsley.

## Notes

- Use dry Marsala, not sweet cooking Marsala.
- Pounding chicken ensures even cooking.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-marsala/

---

# Chicken Noodle Soup

Classic comfort. 30 minutes with rotisserie chicken.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 20 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 medium onion, diced
- 3 carrots, sliced into half-moons
- 3 celery stalks, sliced
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 8 cups chicken broth (good quality)
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 tsp fresh thyme (or ½ tsp dried)
- 1 bay leaf
- 8 oz egg noodles
- 3 cups shredded rotisserie chicken
- 2 tbsp fresh dill or parsley, chopped
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

1. Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
2. Add garlic and tomato paste. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Pour in broth. Add thyme and bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
4. Add noodles. Cook according to package directions (usually 8-10 minutes) until tender.
5. Stir in shredded chicken. Heat through, about 2 minutes.
6. Remove bay leaf. Stir in fresh dill. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

## Notes

- Broth matters: Use the best quality broth you can find. It's the backbone of the soup.
- From scratch: For richer broth, simmer chicken wings with the vegetables for 1 hour before adding noodles.
- Noodle tip: If making ahead, store noodles separately. They'll absorb liquid and get mushy.
- Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Freeze broth and chicken without noodles.

Source: Adapted from Once Upon a Chef
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-noodle-soup/

---

# Chicken Parmesan

Crispy cutlets, bubbling mozzarella. The key: don't bake it in sauce.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 eggs, beaten
- 1½ cups panko breadcrumbs
- ½ cup parmesan, grated
- 1 tsp Italian seasoning
- ½ tsp garlic powder
- ½ tsp salt
- ¼ cup olive oil
- 2 cups marinara sauce
- 8 oz fresh mozzarella, sliced
- Fresh basil for serving

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to broil. Line a baking sheet with foil.
2. Pound chicken to ¼" even thickness between plastic wrap. This ensures even cooking.
3. Set up breading station: flour in one dish, beaten eggs in second, panko mixed with parmesan, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, and salt in third.
4. Dredge each cutlet: flour → egg → panko mixture. Press panko firmly to adhere.
5. Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high. Fry cutlets 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (165°F). Work in batches.
6. Transfer cutlets to baking sheet. Spoon marinara around (not over) chicken—keep the breading exposed.
7. Top each cutlet with mozzarella slices.
8. Broil 2-3 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly. Watch carefully.
9. Serve topped with fresh basil and extra sauce on the side.

## Notes

- The soggy mistake: Most recipes bake chicken IN the sauce. This makes breading soggy. Sauce goes around, not on top.
- Pound it thin: ¼" thickness cooks evenly and stays tender. Thick pieces overcook on outside.
- Fresh mozzarella: Melts better than shredded, creates better texture.
- Make it a sub: Slice and pile on hoagie roll with extra sauce and cheese.
- Storage: Best fresh. Reheat on wire rack in 375°F oven to restore crispness.

Source: Adapted from Natasha's Kitchen
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-parmesan/

---

# Chicken Pot Pie

Creamy filling under flaky pastry

Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs
- 4 tbsp butter
- 1 onion, chopped
- 2 carrots, sliced
- 2 celery ribs, sliced
- ⅓ cup flour
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 cup frozen peas
- 2 tbsp fresh thyme
- 1 sheet puff pastry
- 1 egg, beaten

## Steps

1. Poach chicken in simmering water until cooked, 15-20 minutes. Shred.
2. Melt butter, cook onion, carrots, celery until soft, 8 minutes.
3. Add flour, cook 1 minute. Whisk in broth and cream.
4. Simmer until thickened. Add chicken, peas, thyme. Season well.
5. Transfer to 9x13 dish. Top with puff pastry, cut vents. Brush with egg.
6. Bake at 400°F until golden and bubbling, 25-30 minutes.

## Notes

- Thighs have more flavor than breasts.
- Puff pastry is easier than pie dough.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-pot-pie/

---

# Chicken Quesadilla

Crispy tortilla, melty cheese, seasoned chicken. Ready in 15 minutes.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 cups cooked chicken, shredded
- 1 tsp chili powder
- 1/2 tsp cumin
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 4 large flour tortillas
- 2 cups shredded Mexican cheese blend
- 1/2 cup bell peppers, diced
- 1/4 cup red onion, diced
- 2 Tbsp butter or oil
- Sour cream, salsa, guacamole for serving

## Steps

1. Toss shredded chicken with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, and salt.
2. Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add 1/2 Tbsp butter.
3. Place a tortilla in the skillet. Add 1/2 cup cheese to half the tortilla.
4. Top cheese with 1/4 of the chicken, peppers, and onion. Add more cheese on top.
5. Fold tortilla in half. Cook 2-3 minutes until bottom is golden.
6. Flip carefully and cook 2-3 minutes more until both sides are crispy and cheese is melted.
7. Repeat with remaining ingredients. Cut into triangles and serve with toppings.

## Notes

- Crispy secret: Butter in the pan creates the best golden crust.
- Cheese placement: Cheese on both sides of the filling helps everything stick together.
- Rotisserie shortcut: Use store-bought rotisserie chicken for speed.
- Make it veggie: Swap chicken for black beans and corn.
- Keep warm: Place finished quesadillas on a baking sheet in a 200°F oven while making the rest.

Source: Adapted from Gimme Some Oven
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-quesadilla/

---

# Chicken Salad

Classic, creamy, perfect for sandwiches

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs chicken breasts
- ½ cup mayonnaise
- 2 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 2 celery ribs, chopped fine
- 2 tbsp fresh parsley
- 1 shallot, minced
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cover chicken with salted water. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook until 160°F, 15-20 minutes.
2. Cool 10 minutes, then shred or chop.
3. Whisk mayo, lemon juice, mustard.
4. Add chicken, celery, parsley, shallot. Toss.
5. Season with salt and pepper. Chill 30 minutes.
6. Serve on bread, in lettuce cups, or with crackers.

## Notes

- Poaching keeps chicken moist.
- Shredded has better texture than cubed.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-salad/

---

# Chicken Stir Fry

Tender chicken, crisp vegetables, savory sauce

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs chicken breast, sliced thin
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp cornstarch
- 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine
- 3 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp ginger, minced
- 1 bell pepper, sliced
- 1 cup broccoli
- ½ cup chicken broth
- 2 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil

## Steps

1. Toss chicken with 1 tbsp soy sauce, cornstarch, wine. Marinate 15 minutes.
2. Mix broth, remaining soy sauce, oyster sauce.
3. Heat wok over high until smoking. Add oil.
4. Sear chicken without stirring 1 minute, then stir-fry until just cooked. Transfer out.
5. Stir-fry garlic and ginger 30 seconds. Add vegetables, stir-fry 2 minutes.
6. Return chicken, add sauce. Cook until thickened.
7. Drizzle with sesame oil. Serve over rice.

## Notes

- Velveting (cornstarch marinade) keeps chicken tender.
- Wok must be ripping hot.
- Cook in batches to avoid steaming.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-stir-fry/

---

# Chicken Stock

Rich, golden, foundation of flavor

Makes: 3-4 quarts

## Ingredients

- 4-5 lbs chicken backs, wings, or carcasses
- 1 large onion, quartered
- 2 carrots, chunked
- 2 celery ribs, chunked
- 1 head garlic, halved
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp black peppercorns
- Fresh thyme and parsley stems

## Steps

1. Place chicken parts in large stockpot. Cover with cold water by 2 inches.
2. Bring to boil, reduce to bare simmer. Skim any foam.
3. Add vegetables, garlic, and aromatics.
4. Simmer uncovered 4-6 hours, adding water as needed.
5. Strain through fine-mesh strainer. Discard solids.
6. Cool uncovered. Refrigerate.
7. Skim solidified fat before using.

## Notes

- Never boil - causes cloudy stock.
- More gelatin = richer body.
- Freezes for 6 months.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-stock/

---

# Chicken Tacos

Simple spiced chicken, classic toppings

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- ½ tsp oregano
- Juice of 2 limes
- Corn tortillas
- Diced onion, cilantro, salsa

## Steps

1. Mix oil with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, oregano, 1 tsp salt.
2. Toss chicken with spice mixture. Arrange on baking sheet.
3. Roast at 425°F until cooked and slightly charred, 20-25 minutes.
4. Rest 5 minutes, chop or shred. Toss with lime juice.
5. Warm tortillas. Serve in tortillas with onion, cilantro, salsa.

## Notes

- Thighs stay moister than breasts.
- Char adds taqueria flavor.
- Double-stack corn tortillas.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-tacos/

---

# Chicken Tikka Masala

Charred chicken in creamy tomato sauce

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs chicken thighs
- 1 cup yogurt
- 2 tbsp lemon juice
- 2 tbsp garam masala, divided
- 1 tbsp cumin
- 1 tbsp paprika
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp ginger, minced
- 1 (14 oz) can crushed tomatoes
- 1 cup heavy cream
- Cilantro

## Steps

1. Mix yogurt, lemon juice, 1 tbsp garam masala, cumin, paprika. Marinate chicken 1-4 hours.
2. Broil chicken until charred, 4-5 minutes per side.
3. Cook onion in oil 8 minutes. Add garlic, ginger, remaining garam masala. Cook 1 minute.
4. Add tomatoes, simmer 15 minutes. Stir in cream.
5. Add chicken and juices. Simmer 10 minutes. Garnish with cilantro.
6. Serve with basmati rice and naan.

## Notes

- Yogurt marinade tenderizes.
- Charring under broiler adds authentic flavor.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-tikka-masala/

---

# Chicken Tortilla Soup

Smoky, spicy, with crispy tortilla strips

Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 8 corn tortillas, cut into strips
- Vegetable oil for frying
- 1 onion, chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 jalapeños, minced
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 (14.5 oz) can diced tomatoes
- 8 cups chicken broth
- 1 lb chicken breasts
- ¼ cup cilantro
- Juice of 2 limes
- Avocado, sour cream, cheese

## Steps

1. Fry half the tortilla strips until crisp. Drain, season with salt.
2. Sauté onion 5 minutes. Add remaining tortilla strips, garlic, jalapeños, tomato paste. Cook 2 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes and broth. Simmer.
4. Add chicken breasts, simmer until cooked, 15-20 minutes.
5. Remove chicken, shred, return to pot.
6. Stir in cilantro and lime juice.
7. Serve topped with fried tortillas, avocado, sour cream, cheese.

## Notes

- Cooking tortillas in soup adds body and flavor.
- Crispy strips must be added right before serving.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chicken-tortilla-soup/

---

# Chickpea Cookie Dough

Edible cookie dough with 6g of fiber hiding in it.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Makes: about 1 1/2 cups (6 servings)

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — Nobody guesses the chickpeas. Nobody. The fibermaxxing girlies are right about this one.

_Tested 2026-07. Three full minutes in the processor and a hard chill are what separate 'surprisingly great' from 'hummus with chips.'_

## Ingredients

- 1 can (425g/15 oz) chickpeas, drained, rinsed hard, and patted dry
- 1/3 cup (85g) natural peanut butter (or almond/tahini)
- 3 Tbsp maple syrup
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1/4 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt
- 2 Tbsp oat flour (or blitz oats) - only if needed to thicken
- 1/3 cup mini chocolate chips

## Steps

1. Rinse the chickpeas until the water runs clear, then rub them dry in a clean towel - this also sloughs off some skins. The drier the chickpeas, the less bean the dough tastes.
2. Food-process chickpeas, peanut butter, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt for a full 2-3 minutes, scraping down twice, until completely smooth. Under-processing is why people think this recipe is bad.
3. Too loose? Add oat flour a tablespoon at a time until it scoops like dough.
4. Pulse in the chocolate chips (or stir by hand to keep them whole).
5. Chill 30 minutes - cold is where it stops tasting healthy.
6. Eat with a spoon, stuff into dates, or roll into balls and keep in the fridge up to 5 days.

## Notes

- The math: a can of chickpeas carries roughly 20g fiber and 20g protein across the batch - about 6g fiber per serving with the peanut butter.
- This is dough, not dough-for-baking - no leavening, no egg; it stays dough.
- Natural peanut butter (just peanuts and salt) keeps it scoopable; the sweetened kind makes it stiff and cloying.

## When it goes wrong

- Tastes like hummus → under-processed or wet chickpeas; blitz longer, dry harder.
- Grainy → cheap blender or big-flake skins; peel more skins and process in a food processor, not a bullet.
- Too stiff → dry natural PB from the bottom of the jar; loosen with a teaspoon of milk.
- Too sweet → sweetened peanut butter stacking on the maple; use natural and re-taste before adding syrup.

Source: Fibermaxxing trend, 2026
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chickpea-cookie-dough/

---

# Chili by George

Reliable weeknight chili. Stovetop or slow cooker.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 1½ hrs · Serves: 10

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs ground beef
- 46 oz tomato juice
- 29 oz tomato sauce
- 15 oz kidney beans, drained
- 15 oz pinto beans, drained
- 1½ cups onion, chopped
- ¼ cup bell pepper, chopped
- ¼ cup chili powder
- 1½ tsp cumin
- 1 tsp salt
- ½ tsp oregano
- ½ tsp black pepper
- ½ tsp sugar
- ⅛ tsp cayenne

## Steps

1. Brown ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat until no pink remains, about 8 minutes. Drain the fat.
2. Transfer beef to a large pot. Add tomato juice, tomato sauce, both beans, onion, bell pepper, and all spices. Stir to combine.
3. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce to low and simmer uncovered for 1½ hours, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
4. Taste and adjust salt. Serve with your preferred toppings.

## Notes

- Slow cooker: Brown beef first, then combine everything on low for 8–10 hours.
- Too thin? Swap tomato juice for crushed tomatoes, or simmer uncovered longer.
- Storage: Keeps 5 days refrigerated. Freezes well for 3 months.

Source: Adapted from AllRecipes (3,200+ ratings)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chili-by-george/

---

# Chimichurri

Argentina's iconic herb sauce. Bright, garlicky, and perfect on grilled meat.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Makes: 1 cup

## Ingredients

- 1 cup fresh parsley, packed (flat-leaf preferred)
- 4 cloves garlic
- 2 Tbsp fresh oregano (or 2 tsp dried)
- 1/3 cup red wine vinegar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
- 3/4 cup extra virgin olive oil

## Steps

1. Finely chop parsley, garlic, and oregano by hand or pulse in a food processor. Don't puree—you want texture.
2. Transfer to a bowl. Add red wine vinegar, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Stir to combine.
3. Pour in olive oil and mix well.
4. Let sit at least 10 minutes before serving to let flavors meld. Tastes even better after an hour.

## Notes

- Hand chop vs processor: Hand chopping gives better texture. If using a processor, pulse briefly.
- The vinegar: Red wine vinegar is traditional. Sherry vinegar or lemon juice also work.
- Best on: Grilled steak, chicken, fish, roasted vegetables, crusty bread, eggs.
- Storage: Refrigerate up to 1 week. Bring to room temperature before serving—the oil solidifies when cold.
- Make it ahead: Flavors improve overnight.

Source: Adapted from Downshiftology
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chimichurri/

---

# Chocolate Cake

Rich, moist, deeply chocolatey

Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- Cake: 2 cups flour, 2 cups sugar, ¾ cup cocoa powder, 2 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp salt, 1 cup vegetable oil, 1 cup buttermilk, 2 eggs, 1 cup hot coffee
- Frosting: 1 cup butter, 4 cups powdered sugar, ½ cup cocoa, ¼ cup heavy cream, 2 tsp vanilla

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease two 9-inch pans.
2. Whisk flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, salt.
3. Add oil, buttermilk, eggs. Beat 2 minutes.
4. Stir in hot coffee (batter will be thin).
5. Divide between pans. Bake 30-35 minutes.
6. Cool 10 minutes, turn out onto racks.
7. Frosting: Beat butter until fluffy. Add sugar, cocoa, cream, vanilla.
8. Frost cooled layers.

## Notes

- Hot coffee intensifies chocolate flavor.
- Batter is very thin - that's correct.

Source: Serious Eats (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chocolate-cake/

---

# Chocolate Chip Cookies

Brown butter, toffee notes, crispy edges, chewy center.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 12 min · Makes: 16

## Ingredients

- 1¾ cups all-purpose flour
- ½ tsp baking soda
- 14 tbsp butter (1¾ sticks)
- ½ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup dark brown sugar, packed
- 1 tsp salt
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 whole egg + 1 egg yolk
- 1¼ cups chocolate chips or chunks

## Steps

1. Whisk flour and baking soda in bowl. Set aside.
2. Brown the butter: Melt 10 tbsp butter in skillet over medium-high heat, swirling constantly. Cook until milk solids turn dark golden brown and smell nutty, about 3 minutes. Transfer to large bowl.
3. Add remaining 4 tbsp butter to hot browned butter. Stir until melted.
4. Add both sugars, salt, and vanilla. Whisk until combined.
5. Add egg and yolk. Whisk vigorously for 30 seconds. Let rest 3 minutes. Whisk 30 seconds. Rest 3 minutes. Whisk 30 seconds. (This develops structure.)
6. Fold in flour mixture until just combined. Fold in chocolate chips.
7. Roll dough into 16 balls (about 3 tablespoons each). Place on parchment-lined baking sheets, 2 inches apart.
8. Bake at 375°F for 10-14 minutes until golden brown on edges but still soft in center.
9. Cool on pan 5 minutes, then transfer to rack.

## Notes

- BROWN THE BUTTER: This is the secret. Creates toffee/nutty flavor that's unmistakable.
- Dark brown sugar: More molasses = chewier cookie. Don't substitute light.
- The whisking/resting: Sounds fussy but it dissolves sugar and creates a glossy, crackly top.
- 1 egg + 1 yolk: Extra yolk = chewier texture. Whole eggs make cakier cookies.
- Underbake slightly: They firm up as they cool. Golden edges, soft center = perfect.

Source: Adapted from America's Test Kitchen
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/chocolate-chip-cookies/

---

# Cinnamon Rolls

Soft, gooey, cream cheese frosted

Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- Dough: 1 cup warm milk, 2¼ tsp yeast, ¼ cup sugar, ⅓ cup butter softened, 1 egg, 3½ cups flour, 1 tsp salt
- Filling: ⅓ cup butter softened, ¾ cup brown sugar, 2 tbsp cinnamon
- Glaze: 4 oz cream cheese, ¼ cup butter, 1 cup powdered sugar, ½ tsp vanilla

## Steps

1. Dissolve yeast in warm milk with pinch sugar. Let sit 5 minutes.
2. Mix in sugar, butter, egg. Add flour and salt. Knead until smooth, 8 minutes.
3. Let rise until doubled, 1 hour.
4. Roll to 16x12 rectangle. Spread with butter. Mix brown sugar and cinnamon, sprinkle over.
5. Roll up tightly from long side. Cut into 12 pieces.
6. Place in greased 9x13 pan. Let rise 30 minutes.
7. Bake at 350°F for 25-30 minutes.
8. Beat glaze ingredients. Spread on warm rolls.

## Notes

- Warm (not hot) milk activates yeast.
- Use dental floss to cut dough.
- Don't overbake.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cinnamon-rolls/

---

# New England Clam Chowder

Creamy, briny, and loaded with clams and potatoes. The real deal.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 35 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 4 slices thick-cut bacon, diced
- 2 Tbsp butter
- 1 medium onion, diced
- 2 stalks celery, diced
- 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour
- 2 cups clam juice (from cans + bottled)
- 2 cups whole milk
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1.5 lbs Yukon gold potatoes, diced 1/2 inch
- 2 cans (6.5 oz each) chopped clams, drained (reserve juice)
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp fresh thyme
- Salt and white pepper to taste
- Fresh chives for garnish

## Steps

1. Cook bacon in a large Dutch oven over medium heat until crispy. Remove to a paper towel, leaving fat in pot.
2. Add butter to bacon fat. Sauté onion and celery 5 minutes until softened.
3. Sprinkle flour over vegetables. Stir and cook 2 minutes.
4. Slowly whisk in clam juice, then milk. Add bay leaves and thyme.
5. Add potatoes. Bring to a simmer and cook 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
6. Stir in heavy cream and clams. Heat through 5 minutes—don't boil or clams get rubbery.
7. Remove bay leaves. Season with salt and white pepper.
8. Serve topped with reserved bacon and fresh chives.

## Notes

- Clam juice: Use the liquid from the canned clams plus bottled clam juice to reach 2 cups.
- Fresh clams: Steam 3 lbs littlenecks. Chop meat, strain and use the cooking liquid.
- Potatoes: Yukon Golds hold their shape. Russets make it creamier but can get mushy.
- White pepper: Traditional for chowder. Black pepper works but adds visible specks.
- Make ahead: Flavors improve overnight. Reheat gently.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/clam-chowder/

---

# Coleslaw

Creamy, tangy, classic BBQ side

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 head green cabbage, shredded (8 cups)
- 2 carrots, shredded
- ¾ cup mayonnaise
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar
- 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
- ½ tsp celery seed
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Toss cabbage and carrots.
2. Whisk mayo, sugar, vinegar, mustard, celery seed.
3. Pour over cabbage. Toss to coat.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Refrigerate at least 1 hour.
6. Toss again before serving.

## Notes

- Salting cabbage first draws out moisture.
- Letting it sit melds flavors.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/coleslaw/

---

# Corn on the Cob

Summer in a bite. Three ways: boiled, grilled, or roasted.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 6 ears fresh corn, husked
- 4 Tbsp butter
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Optional: chili powder, lime, parmesan, fresh herbs

## Steps

1. BOILED: Bring a large pot of water to boil. Don't add salt—it toughens the corn. Add corn and cook 4-6 minutes until tender. Drain.
2. GRILLED: Preheat grill to medium-high. Brush corn with oil. Grill 10-12 minutes, turning every few minutes, until charred in spots.
3. ROASTED: Preheat oven to 400°F. Place corn directly on oven rack. Roast 20-25 minutes until tender.
4. Serve immediately with butter, salt, and pepper.

## Notes

- Freshness: Fresh corn is best eaten within 1-2 days. The sugar converts to starch over time.
- No salt in water: Salt toughens corn. Season after cooking.
- Test for doneness: Pierce a kernel with your fingernail—it should be tender and release milky juice.
- Mexican street corn (elote): Coat grilled corn with mayo, cotija cheese, chili powder, and lime.
- Cut off the cob: Stand ear upright in a bowl and slice down with a sharp knife.

Source: Adapted from Simply Recipes
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/corn-on-the-cob/

---

# Southern Cornbread

Cast iron, crispy edges, no sugar. The way it should be.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1½ cups cornmeal (stone-ground if you can find it)
- ½ cup all-purpose flour
- 1 tbsp baking powder
- ½ tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1½ cups buttermilk
- 1 egg
- 10 tbsp butter, divided

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Place 10-inch cast iron skillet in oven while it heats.
2. Whisk cornmeal, flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in large bowl.
3. Melt 6 tablespoons butter. Whisk with buttermilk and egg in another bowl.
4. Pour wet ingredients into dry. Stir until just combined. Don't overmix.
5. Carefully remove hot skillet from oven. Add remaining 4 tablespoons butter, swirl to coat.
6. Immediately pour batter into sizzling butter. It should sizzle loudly.
7. Bake 20-25 minutes until golden brown and toothpick comes out clean.
8. Brush top with more butter if desired. Let cool 5 minutes before slicing.

## Notes

- HOT skillet + butter = crispy crust: This is non-negotiable. The sizzle when batter hits the pan creates the crust.
- No sugar: Southern cornbread is savory. If you want sweet, that's Northern-style (and that's fine, just different).
- Cast iron matters: Retains heat, creates crispy edges. A regular pan won't give the same results.
- Bacon grease: For extra flavor, replace some butter with bacon drippings.
- Serve with: Chili, beans, collard greens, or crumbled into buttermilk (yes, really).

Source: Adapted from The Kitchn
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cornbread/

---

# Cottage Cheese Flatbread

The viral flourless version: cottage cheese, eggs, an oven.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 35 min · Makes: 1 large flatbread (2 servings)

**Verdict: OVERHYPED** — A solid high-protein wrap, but anyone calling this 'bread' is lying to you - it's a flat baked omelet.

_Tested 2026-07. Made the flourless viral version (cottage cheese + eggs, baked) the primary recipe; the old skillet flour-dough version moved to a note._

## Ingredients

- 1 cup (225g) full-fat cottage cheese
- 2 large eggs
- 1/4 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (a pinch of table salt)
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder (optional)
- 1/2 tsp Italian herbs or everything bagel seasoning (optional)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a sheet pan with parchment and mist the parchment lightly with oil - this thing loves to stick.
2. Blend cottage cheese, eggs, salt, and seasonings until completely smooth, about 30 seconds. Lumps bake into rubbery pockets.
3. Pour onto the parchment and spread into a thin rectangle, roughly 8x11 inches and 1/4 inch thick. Thinner at the edges = burnt lace; keep it even.
4. Bake 30-40 minutes until golden brown across the surface and fully set in the center - the eggs should be completely cooked with no wet sheen (an instant-read in the middle will read 160°F+).
5. Cool 5 minutes, then peel off the parchment. Warm, it's flexible enough to fold and roll.
6. Use as a wrap, or top like a pizza and broil 2-3 minutes until the cheese melts.

## Notes
