- Full-fat cottage cheese only. Nonfat weeps water and bakes up rubbery.
- This is not bread and won't fool anyone who's had bread. It IS a genuinely useful low-carb, high-protein wrap that holds fillings without cracking.
- Want an actual chewy flatbread instead? The pre-viral 2-ingredient dough version: blend 1 cup (225g) cottage cheese smooth, mix with 1/2 cup (60g) all-purpose flour and 1/4 tsp salt into a sticky dough, roll thin with plenty of flour, and cook in an oiled skillet 2-3 minutes per side. More carbs, more bread.

## When it goes wrong

- Stuck to the parchment → you skipped oiling it, or peeled while piping hot; grease the paper and give it 5 minutes.
- Wet, eggy middle → underbaked or spread too thick; it needs to be golden and set edge to edge.
- Rubbery texture → nonfat cottage cheese or unblended lumps; use full-fat and blend fully smooth.
- Cracks when rolled → overbaked or rolled cold; wrap while still warm.

Source: TikTok viral recipe
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cottage-cheese-flatbread/

---

# Cottage Cheese Taco Shells

The protein taco shell that ate TikTok this summer.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 35 min · Serves: 3 (6 tacos)

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — Legitimately good protein delivery - just don't expect a corn tortilla.

_Tested 2026-07. Rack-draping is what separates a taco from a sad fold - don't skip the second bake._

## Ingredients

**For the shells:**
- 1 cup (225g) full-fat cottage cheese
- 4 large egg whites (or 2 whole eggs)
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp smoked paprika
- 1/4 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt
**For the filling:**
- 1 lb ground chicken
- 1 Tbsp olive oil
- 1 Tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp dried oregano
- 3/4 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt
- 1/3 cup water
- To serve: shredded lettuce, pico de gallo, hot sauce, lime

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line two sheet pans with parchment and mist with oil - these shells stick with intent.
2. Blend cottage cheese, egg whites, garlic powder, paprika, and salt until completely smooth, about 30 seconds.
3. Pour into six 5-inch rounds, about 1/4 cup each, and spread thin and even with the back of a spoon.
4. Bake 20-25 minutes until set and golden at the edges with no wet shine in the center.
5. Meanwhile, cook the filling: heat oil over medium-high, brown the chicken, breaking it up, 5-6 minutes. Add spices and salt, stir 30 seconds, add water, and simmer 2-3 minutes until saucy. Chicken should hit 165°F.
6. Peel shells off the parchment while warm. To crisp them taco-shaped, drape each over two bars of the oven rack and bake 4-5 more minutes.
7. Fill and eat immediately - shells soften as they sit.

## Notes

- Full-fat cottage cheese only. Nonfat weeps and turns rubbery.
- Whole eggs make a sturdier, eggier shell; whites keep it lighter and more neutral.
- These are wraps in taco cosplay - flexible when warm, crisp only if you rack-bake them.

## When it goes wrong

- Shells stuck → parchment wasn't oiled or peeled cold; grease the paper, peel warm.
- Rubbery → nonfat cottage cheese or unblended lumps; full-fat, blended smooth.
- Cracked when folded → overbaked or filled cold; fill while warm.
- Watery filling → skipped the simmer; cook until the water reduces to a coating.

Source: TikTok cottage cheese wave, summer 2026
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cottage-cheese-tacos/

---

# Crab Cakes

Minimal filler, maximum crab

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb lump crabmeat
- ¼ cup mayonnaise
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire
- 1 tbsp Old Bay
- 2 tbsp parsley
- ½ cup panko
- 2 tbsp butter
- Lemon and tartar sauce

## Steps

1. Pick through crabmeat for shells, keeping lumps intact.
2. Whisk mayo, egg yolk, mustard, Worcestershire, Old Bay, parsley.
3. Gently fold in crabmeat and ¼ cup panko. Don't break up lumps.
4. Form into 4 patties. Coat with remaining panko.
5. Refrigerate 30 minutes.
6. Cook in butter over medium until golden, 3-4 minutes per side.
7. Serve with lemon and tartar sauce.

## Notes

- Best crabmeat = best cakes.
- Handle gently to preserve lumps.
- Chilling firms them up.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/crab-cakes/

---

# Smashed Cucumber Salad

The viral crunchy, garlicky, spicy cucumber situation.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 4

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — Five minutes of work for the best thing on the table - the 500M views are deserved.

_Tested 2026-07. Verified the salt-and-drain step is non-negotiable - skipping it waters the dressing down in minutes._

## Ingredients

- 4 Persian cucumbers (or 2 English cucumbers)
- 1 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (for draining)
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 Tbsp soy sauce
- 1 Tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 Tbsp sesame oil
- 1-2 Tbsp chili crisp or chili oil
- 1 tsp sugar
- Sesame seeds for garnish
- Fresh cilantro (optional)

## Steps

1. Place cucumbers on cutting board. Using the flat side of a knife or cleaver, smash each cucumber firmly until it cracks and splits. Cut into rough 1-inch pieces.
2. Toss cucumber pieces with salt in a colander. Let drain 10-15 minutes to draw out excess water. This keeps the salad from getting soggy.
3. While cucumbers drain, whisk together garlic, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, chili crisp, and sugar.
4. Pat cucumbers dry with paper towels. Transfer to serving bowl.
5. Pour dressing over cucumbers and toss well, making sure sauce gets into all the cracks and crevices.
6. Garnish with sesame seeds and cilantro. Serve immediately for maximum crunch.

## Notes

- Smashing is better than cutting - the rough edges absorb more sauce.
- Salting and draining is crucial. Skip this and you get a watery salad.
- Best eaten within 30 minutes. The cucumbers will continue to release water.

## When it goes wrong

- Watery puddle in the bowl → skipped the salt-drain, or it sat too long; drain 15 minutes and eat within 30.
- Not crunchy → wrong cucumber; Persian or English only - regular slicers are all seeds and water.
- Too fiery → chili crisp overload; start with 1 Tbsp, and know the oil spreads heat more than the flakes.

Source: Chinese pai huang gua
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/cucumber-salad/

---

# Deviled Eggs

The party appetizer that disappears first. Every. Single. Time.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 12 min · Makes: 24 halves

## Ingredients

- 12 large eggs
- 1/3 cup mayonnaise
- 1 Tbsp Dijon mustard
- 1 Tbsp yellow mustard
- 1 Tbsp white vinegar or pickle juice
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/8 tsp black pepper
- Paprika for garnish
- Fresh chives for garnish

## Steps

1. Place eggs in a single layer in a saucepan. Cover with cold water by 1 inch.
2. Bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Remove from heat, cover, and let sit 12 minutes.
3. Transfer eggs to an ice bath for 5 minutes. Peel under cool running water.
4. Slice eggs in half lengthwise. Gently remove yolks to a bowl.
5. Mash yolks with a fork. Add mayo, both mustards, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Mix until smooth.
6. Pipe or spoon filling into egg white halves.
7. Sprinkle with paprika and chives. Refrigerate until serving.

## Notes

- Easy peel: Older eggs (1-2 weeks old) peel much easier than fresh eggs.
- Steam method: Steam eggs 12 minutes instead of boiling for easier peeling.
- Pipe it: Use a zip-lock bag with the corner cut off for a cleaner look.
- Make ahead: Fill eggs up to 24 hours ahead. Cover tightly with plastic wrap.
- Variations: Add crumbled bacon, relish, hot sauce, or everything bagel seasoning.

Source: Adapted from The Kitchn
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/deviled-eggs/

---

# Dinner Rolls

Soft, buttery, pull-apart

Serves: 16

## Ingredients

- 1 cup warm milk
- 2¼ tsp active dry yeast
- ⅓ cup sugar
- ⅓ cup butter, softened
- 2 eggs
- 4 cups flour
- 1 tsp salt
- 2 tbsp butter, melted (for brushing)

## Steps

1. Dissolve yeast in warm milk with pinch sugar. Let sit 5 minutes.
2. Add sugar, butter, eggs. Mix well.
3. Add flour and salt. Knead until smooth and elastic, 8-10 minutes.
4. Place in greased bowl. Cover, let rise until doubled, 1 hour.
5. Punch down. Divide into 16 pieces. Roll into balls.
6. Place in greased 9x13 pan. Cover, let rise 30 minutes.
7. Bake at 375°F until golden, 20-25 minutes.
8. Brush with melted butter immediately.

## Notes

- Dough should be slightly tacky.
- Butter while hot so it soaks in.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/dinner-rolls/

---

# Dubai Chocolate Bar

The viral pistachio-kunafa chocolate, made with the right filling.

Prep: 30 min · Cook: 10 min · Makes: 8 bars

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The crunchy pistachio filling deserves the hype - just know the jar of pistachio cream costs more than the chocolate.

_Tested 2026-07. Replaced the old tahini-honey filling with actual pistachio cream (plus a DIY blend), switched kataifi to skillet-toasting in butter, and added a real temper option._

## Ingredients

- 300g (10.5 oz) milk chocolate, chopped (dark works, but the original is milk)
**For the filling:**
- 100g kataifi pastry (shredded phyllo), roughly chopped
- 45g (3 Tbsp) unsalted butter
- 250g (about 3/4 cup) pistachio cream (sweetened 'crema di pistacchio' spread - not unsweetened pistachio butter)
- 1 Tbsp tahini (optional - loosens the filling and cuts the sweetness)
- 1/4 tsp fine salt

## Steps

1. Toast the kataifi: melt butter in a wide skillet over medium heat, add chopped kataifi, and stir constantly 5-8 minutes until deep golden brown and crisp everywhere. Pale kataifi = soggy filling. Cool completely.
2. Stir pistachio cream, tahini, and salt together, then fold in the cooled kataifi. It should look like green granola bound in cream, still crunchy.
3. Decide on your chocolate path. Option A, temper (bars snap and survive room temp): melt two-thirds of the chocolate to 113°F (45°C), pull off heat, stir in the remaining third until it cools to 84°F (29°C), then rewarm briefly to 86°F (30°C). Option B, just melt it in 30-second microwave bursts - but then the bars must live in the fridge.
4. Line a 9x5 loaf pan with parchment (or use a chocolate bar mold). Pour in half the chocolate and spread up the sides a little. Chill 5-10 minutes until just set.
5. Pack the pistachio-kataifi filling evenly over the chocolate, leaving a small border. Press it flat.
6. Pour remaining chocolate over the top and spread to seal the edges completely. Tap the pan to release air bubbles.
7. Set: tempered bars set at cool room temperature in about an hour; untempered, refrigerate at least 2 hours. Slice with a sharp knife dipped in hot water and wiped dry.

## Notes

- No pistachio cream? Make it: blend 150g shelled roasted unsalted pistachios with 3 Tbsp neutral oil, 50g (1/2 cup) powdered sugar, and a pinch of salt until as smooth as your machine allows. It'll be coarser than the jarred stuff but tastes right. Do not substitute tahini and honey - that's a sesame bar.
- The temper-or-refrigerate tradeoff: untempered chocolate blooms (dull gray streaks) within a day or two at room temperature. Bloom is ugly but harmless. Temper if you're gifting; refrigerate if you're eating.
- Kataifi is in the freezer case at Middle Eastern groceries. Bars keep 2 weeks refrigerated; the kataifi slowly softens after the first few days.

## When it goes wrong

- Filling turned soggy by day two → kataifi was undertoasted; it needs to be deep golden and fully crisp before mixing.
- Gray streaks on the chocolate → bloom from untempered chocolate stored at room temp; refrigerate untempered bars or temper next time.
- Filling oozes when sliced → too warm or too much tahini; chill 20 minutes and cut with a hot dry knife.
- Top shell separated from the filling → filling was mounded unevenly or chocolate too cool when poured; press the filling flat and pour promptly.

Source: Inspired by Fix Dessert Chocolatier
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/dubai-chocolate-bar/

---

# Eggs Benedict

Poached eggs, Canadian bacon, hollandaise

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 English muffins, split and toasted
- 8 slices Canadian bacon
- 8 eggs
- 2 tbsp white vinegar
- Hollandaise: 3 egg yolks, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 cup (2 sticks) butter, melted, pinch cayenne

## Steps

1. Make hollandaise: Blend yolks and lemon juice 5 seconds. With blender running, slowly drizzle in hot butter until thick. Season with salt and cayenne. Keep warm.
2. Heat Canadian bacon in skillet until browned.
3. Bring water to bare simmer. Add vinegar.
4. Crack eggs into small bowls. Swirl water, slip eggs in. Poach 3-4 minutes.
5. Remove eggs with slotted spoon, drain.
6. Assemble: muffin, bacon, egg, hollandaise.

## Notes

- Blender hollandaise is foolproof.
- Fresh eggs poach better.
- Hollandaise breaks if too hot.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/eggs-benedict/

---

# Fish and Chips

Crispy beer batter, double-fried chips

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs cod or haddock
- 1½ cups flour, divided
- ½ cup cornstarch
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1 cup cold beer
- 2 lbs russet potatoes
- Vegetable oil for frying
- Malt vinegar and tartar sauce

## Steps

1. Cut potatoes into ½-inch fries. Soak in cold water 1 hour.
2. Heat oil to 325°F. Drain and dry potatoes. Fry 5 minutes until pale. Drain.
3. Increase oil to 375°F. Fry potatoes again until golden, 3-5 minutes. Season.
4. Whisk 1 cup flour, cornstarch, baking powder, 1 tsp salt. Whisk in beer.
5. Cut fish into strips. Dredge in remaining flour.
6. Dip in batter. Fry until golden, 4-5 minutes. Drain, season.
7. Serve with malt vinegar and tartar sauce.

## Notes

- Double-frying = crispy chips.
- Cold beer keeps batter light.
- Soaking removes starch.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/fish-and-chips/

---

# Fish Tacos

Spiced fish, tangy crema, fresh slaw

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs firm white fish (mahi, cod, tilapia)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- ½ cup mayonnaise
- 2 tbsp lime juice
- 1 tsp hot sauce
- 2 cups shredded cabbage
- Corn tortillas
- Cilantro, lime, salsa

## Steps

1. Mix mayo, lime juice, hot sauce for crema.
2. Mix chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, 1 tsp salt.
3. Pat fish dry. Cut into strips. Toss with oil and spices.
4. Cook fish over high heat 2-3 minutes per side until just cooked.
5. Warm tortillas.
6. Serve fish in tortillas with cabbage, crema, cilantro, lime.

## Notes

- Mahi or cod hold up best.
- Don't overcook - fish should just flake.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/fish-tacos/

---

# French Onion Soup

Rich caramelized onions, beefy broth, bubbly Gruyère

Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 4 tbsp unsalted butter
- 4 large yellow onions, thinly sliced
- 4 cloves garlic, smashed
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 sprigs fresh thyme
- ½ bottle dry red wine
- 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
- 8 cups low-sodium beef broth
- 1 baguette, sliced 1-inch thick
- 1 lb Gruyère cheese, grated
- Kosher salt and black pepper

## Steps

1. Melt butter in Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onions and garlic, cook 25 minutes until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
2. Add bay leaves, thyme, and wine. Simmer until wine evaporates, about 5 minutes.
3. Dust with flour, stir to coat. Cook 2 minutes on medium-low.
4. Add broth, bring to simmer. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer 20-30 minutes.
5. Toast baguette slices under broiler until golden.
6. Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Top with bread and generous Gruyère.
7. Broil until cheese is bubbly and golden, 2-3 minutes.

## Notes

- Don't rush caramelization - low and slow builds depth.
- Mix Swiss and Gruyère if budget is tight.
- Red wine gives deeper color; white works too.

Source: Tyler Florence / Food Network (1,000+ five-star reviews)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/french-onion-soup/

---

# French Toast

Custardy center, golden exterior

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 8 slices thick brioche or challah
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup whole milk
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp vanilla
- ½ tsp cinnamon
- Pinch salt
- 2 tbsp butter
- Maple syrup and powdered sugar

## Steps

1. Whisk eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, salt.
2. Heat griddle over medium. Add butter.
3. Dip bread in custard, 20-30 seconds per side.
4. Cook until golden, 3-4 minutes per side.
5. Add more butter between batches.
6. Keep warm in 200°F oven.
7. Serve with maple syrup and powdered sugar.

## Notes

- Thick bread works best.
- Day-old bread absorbs better.
- Don't over-soak.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/french-toast/

---

# Southern Fried Chicken

Buttermilk-brined, shatteringly crispy. Worth every step.

Prep: 30 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- Buttermilk Brine: 3-4 lbs chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, breasts), 2 cups buttermilk, 2 tbsp kosher salt, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp cayenne
- Dredge: 2 cups all-purpose flour, ½ cup cornstarch, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tsp cayenne, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper
- For Frying: Vegetable oil or peanut oil (enough for 1" depth in pan)

## Steps

1. Brine: Whisk buttermilk, salt, paprika, garlic powder, and cayenne. Submerge chicken pieces. Refrigerate at least 6 hours, preferably overnight.
2. Mix dredge: Combine flour, cornstarch, paprika, garlic powder, cayenne, salt, and pepper in a wide bowl.
3. Double dredge: Remove chicken from buttermilk (don't shake off excess). Coat in flour mixture. Dip back in buttermilk. Coat in flour again. Let sit on rack 10 minutes.
4. Heat oil to 350°F in Dutch oven or deep skillet. Use a thermometer.
5. Fry in batches (don't crowd): Dark meat 13-15 minutes, breasts 15-18 minutes. Flip halfway. Internal temp should reach 165°F.
6. Drain on wire rack (not paper towels—they make it soggy). Season with pinch of salt immediately.

## Notes

- Cornstarch = extra crispy: It creates a shatteringly crunchy coating. Don't skip it.
- Double dredge: The second coating is what gives you that craggy, crispy exterior.
- Don't crowd: Too much chicken drops oil temp = greasy, soggy coating.
- Wire rack: Draining on paper towels traps steam and makes bottom soggy.
- Storage: Best fresh. Reheat in 400°F oven on wire rack to re-crisp.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats Food Lab
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/fried-chicken/

---

# Frozen Yogurt Clusters

Chocolate-shelled frozen yogurt bites. The freezer snack of the summer.

Prep: 15 min (plus 2 hr freeze) · Cook: 0 min · Makes: 12 clusters

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — Fifteen minutes of work, a freezer bag of snacks that beat anything in the ice cream aisle for protein.

_Tested 2026-07. Cooling the melted chocolate before dipping is the difference between a shell and a milkshake._

## Ingredients

- 1 cup (225g) full-fat Greek yogurt
- 1 1/2 Tbsp honey
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- Pinch of fine salt
- 1 cup mixed berries, chopped if large (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)
- 150g (5 oz) dark chocolate, chopped
- 1 tsp coconut oil
- Flaky salt (optional, correct)

## Steps

1. Stir yogurt, honey, vanilla, and salt together. Fold in the berries gently - streaks are fine, puree is not.
2. Scoop 12 mounds (about 2 Tbsp each) onto a parchment-lined sheet pan that fits your freezer.
3. Freeze at least 2 hours, until completely solid.
4. Melt chocolate and coconut oil in 30-second microwave bursts, stirring between, until just smooth. Let it cool 2-3 minutes - hot chocolate melts frozen yogurt on contact.
5. Working fast and in batches (keep the rest in the freezer), dip the frozen mounds to coat, or spoon chocolate over the tops and let it run down. Set each back on the parchment.
6. Sprinkle with flaky salt. Freeze 10 minutes to set the shell.
7. Store in a freezer bag. Let sit 2-3 minutes before eating - straight from the freezer they're a dental event.

## Notes

- Coconut oil thins the chocolate into a thin, snappy shell - the Magic Shell principle.
- Frozen yogurt is icier than ice cream; the honey and full-fat yogurt keep it scoopable-adjacent.
- Any fruit works. Banana slices in the yogurt make them creamier.

## When it goes wrong

- Chocolate slid right off → yogurt not frozen solid or chocolate too hot; freeze hard, cool the dip.
- Shell won't snap → no coconut oil or chocolate chips with stabilizers; use real chopped chocolate plus the oil.
- Icy, rock-hard bites → nonfat yogurt or no honey; both are doing anti-freeze work here.
- Clusters fused in the bag → bagged before the shell set; give them the final 10-minute freeze.

Source: TikTok viral, summer 2026
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/frozen-yogurt-clusters/

---

# Garlic Bread

Buttery, garlicky, and crispy on the outside. The perfect side for pasta night.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 12 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 loaf French or Italian bread
- 1/2 cup (1 stick) butter, softened
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 Tbsp fresh parsley, minced
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/4 cup parmesan cheese, grated (optional)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Slice bread in half lengthwise.
3. Mix softened butter with garlic, parsley, salt, and parmesan if using.
4. Spread garlic butter generously on cut sides of bread.
5. Place bread cut-side up on a baking sheet.
6. Bake 10-12 minutes until edges are golden and butter is bubbling.
7. For extra crispy, broil 1-2 minutes. Watch carefully.
8. Slice and serve immediately.

## Notes

- The bread: French baguette, Italian loaf, or ciabatta all work. Use day-old bread for extra crunch.
- Garlic options: Roasted garlic for a milder, sweeter flavor. Raw for more punch.
- Cheesy version: Add mozzarella on top for the last 3 minutes of baking.
- Make ahead: Prepare garlic butter up to 1 week ahead. Refrigerate.
- Freezer friendly: Spread butter on bread and freeze. Bake from frozen, adding a few minutes.

Source: Adapted from RecipeTin Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/garlic-bread/

---

# Garlic Butter Shrimp

15-minute dinner. Butter, garlic, shrimp. That's it. That's the recipe.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 8 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled and deveined
- 4 Tbsp butter
- 6 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/4 cup dry white wine (or chicken broth)
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 2 Tbsp fresh parsley, chopped

## Steps

1. Pat shrimp dry with paper towels. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 2 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink. Remove to a plate.
4. Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter and garlic. Sauté 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Pour in wine and lemon juice. Let bubble 1-2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits.
6. Return shrimp to the pan. Toss to coat in the sauce. Add red pepper flakes.
7. Remove from heat. Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.

## Notes

- Don't overcook: Shrimp go from perfect to rubbery in seconds. Pull them when just pink.
- Size matters: Large or jumbo shrimp work best. Small shrimp overcook too easily.
- Dry the shrimp: Wet shrimp steam instead of sear. Pat them very dry.
- Serve with: Angel hair pasta, rice, crusty bread, or over zucchini noodles.
- No wine: Substitute chicken broth with a splash of extra lemon juice.

Source: Adapted from Cafe Delites
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/garlic-butter-shrimp/

---

# Glazed Ham

Holiday centerpiece, sweet and sticky

Serves: 12-15

## Ingredients

- 1 bone-in spiral-sliced ham (8-10 lbs)
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ¼ cup honey
- 2 tbsp Dijon mustard
- ¼ tsp cloves
- ¼ tsp cinnamon

## Steps

1. Place ham cut-side down in roasting pan.
2. Cover tightly with foil. Bake at 250°F until 100°F internal, about 1.5-2 hours.
3. Mix glaze ingredients.
4. Remove foil. Increase oven to 350°F.
5. Brush with half the glaze. Bake 15 minutes.
6. Brush with remaining glaze. Bake until 120°F and caramelized, 15-20 minutes.
7. Rest 15 minutes.

## Notes

- Spiral ham is already cooked - just heating and glazing.
- Cut-side down keeps meat moist.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/glazed-ham/

---

# Gravy

Silky, savory, essential for meat

Makes: 3 cups

## Ingredients

- 4 tbsp butter (or pan drippings)
- ¼ cup flour
- 3 cups chicken or turkey stock
- Pan drippings (optional)
- Fresh thyme
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Melt butter in saucepan over medium heat.
2. Whisk in flour. Cook, whisking constantly, until light brown, 2-3 minutes.
3. Gradually whisk in stock, scraping up any browned bits.
4. Add thyme. Simmer, whisking occasionally, until thickened, 10-15 minutes.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Strain if desired.
7. Add any pan drippings from roasted meat.

## Notes

- Cooking flour removes raw taste.
- Gradual addition prevents lumps.
- Strain for silky texture.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/gravy/

---

# Greek Salad

Horiatiki - the real deal, no lettuce required.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 4

_Tested 2026-07. Verified as written - the block-not-crumbled feta and no-lettuce calls are correct horiatiki._

## Ingredients

- 4 ripe tomatoes, cut in wedges
- 1 English cucumber, halved and sliced thick
- 1 small red onion, thinly sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 1/2 cup Kalamata olives
- 200g (7 oz) feta cheese, in one block
- 1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil
- 1 Tbsp red wine vinegar
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cut tomatoes into wedges, cucumbers into half-moons, peppers into rings, and onion into thin slices.
2. Arrange vegetables on a platter or in a wide shallow bowl. Scatter olives over top.
3. Place the block of feta directly on top. Don't crumble it - that's not traditional.
4. Drizzle generously with olive oil and a splash of red wine vinegar.
5. Sprinkle oregano, salt, and pepper over everything.
6. Serve immediately with crusty bread to soak up the juices.

## Notes

- Traditional Greek salad has no lettuce. Just chunky vegetables.
- Use the best olive oil you have - it's doing a lot of work here.
- The feta should be a whole slab, not crumbled. Each person breaks off what they want.

## When it goes wrong

- Watery mess → mealy out-of-season tomatoes; this is a summer salad, don't fight it in January.
- Harsh raw onion → slice thinner, or soak slices in cold water 10 minutes.
- Bland → cheap oil or timid seasoning; the olive oil is an ingredient here, not a garnish.

Source: Traditional Greek
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/greek-salad/

---

# Green Bean Casserole

Creamy, crunchy, holiday favorite

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs fresh green beans, trimmed
- 8 oz cremini mushrooms, sliced
- 3 tbsp butter
- 3 tbsp flour
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 tsp soy sauce
- 1½ cups fried onions (French's)
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Blanch green beans in salted water 4 minutes. Ice bath. Drain well.
2. Melt butter. Cook mushrooms until browned, 8 minutes. Season.
3. Add flour, cook 1 minute. Whisk in broth and cream.
4. Simmer until thickened, 5 minutes. Add soy sauce.
5. Combine beans and sauce in 9x13 dish.
6. Top with 1 cup fried onions.
7. Bake at 375°F for 25 minutes. Add remaining onions. Bake 5 more minutes.

## Notes

- Fresh beans are so much better than canned.
- Adding onions at end keeps them crispy.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/green-bean-casserole/

---

# Grilled Chicken Breast

Juicy, not dry. Finally.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 12 min · Serves: 4

_Tested 2026-07. Specified salt type and doubled the amount - 1 tsp unspecified salt for 2 lb of chicken was underseasoned with kosher._

## Ingredients

- 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts
- 2 Tbsp olive oil
- 2 tsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (1 tsp if table salt)
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp dried oregano

## Steps

1. Pound chicken to even 3/4-inch thickness. This is the most important step for even cooking.
2. Mix salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and oregano. Coat chicken with oil, then season both sides.
3. Let chicken sit at room temperature 15-20 minutes while grill heats.
4. Preheat grill to medium-high (400-450°F). Clean and oil grates.
5. Grill chicken 5-6 minutes per side. Don't move it - let it develop grill marks.
6. Check internal temp - pull at 160°F (carryover will bring it to 165°F).
7. Rest 5 minutes before slicing. This redistributes the juices.

## Notes

- Pounding to even thickness is non-negotiable. Thick parts overcook while thin parts dry out.
- Use an instant-read thermometer. Guessing = dry chicken.
- Resting is not optional. Cut too early and all the juice runs out.

## When it goes wrong

- Dry chicken → no thermometer, or skipped pounding; pull at 160°F and let carryover finish it.
- Burnt outside, raw inside → grill too hot or uneven thickness; 400-450°F and pound to 3/4 inch.
- Stuck to the grates → grates dirty, cold, or dry; clean, preheat, oil, and don't flip until it releases.

Source: rcpbx original
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/grilled-chicken/

---

# Guacamole

Chunky, fresh, perfect every time

Makes: 2 cups

## Ingredients

- 3 ripe avocados
- 1 lime, juiced
- ½ tsp salt
- ½ cup cilantro, chopped
- ¼ cup white onion, minced
- 1 jalapeño, seeded and minced
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1 tomato, diced (optional)

## Steps

1. Halve avocados, remove pit. Scoop into bowl.
2. Add lime juice and salt. Mash to desired consistency.
3. Fold in cilantro, onion, jalapeño, garlic.
4. Add tomato if using.
5. Taste and adjust lime and salt.
6. Press plastic wrap directly on surface. Serve within a few hours.

## Notes

- Ripe avocados are essential.
- Lime juice prevents browning.
- Salt brings out flavor - don't skimp.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/guacamole/

---

# Hash Browns

Shredded, golden, shatteringly crispy

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs russet potatoes
- 3 tbsp butter or bacon fat
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Peel potatoes. Shred on box grater.
2. Rinse until water runs clear. Drain well.
3. Wrap in towel and squeeze out all moisture.
4. Heat butter in cast iron over medium.
5. Add potatoes in even layer. Season. Press flat.
6. Cook without stirring until bottom is golden and crispy, 8-10 minutes.
7. Flip in sections. Cook until second side is crispy, 8-10 minutes.

## Notes

- Rinsing removes starch for crispiness.
- Dry, dry, dry.
- Don't touch them while cooking.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/hash-browns/

---

# Hollandaise Sauce

The silky, lemony butter sauce that makes eggs Benedict legendary.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 10 min · Makes: 1 cup

## Ingredients

- 3 large egg yolks
- 1 Tbsp lemon juice
- 1/2 cup (1 stick) butter, melted and hot
- Pinch of cayenne pepper
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1 Tbsp warm water (if needed)

## Steps

1. Whisk egg yolks and lemon juice in a heatproof bowl until slightly thickened.
2. Set bowl over a pot of barely simmering water (don't let bowl touch water).
3. Whisk constantly until yolks are warm and slightly thickened, about 2 minutes.
4. Remove from heat. Very slowly drizzle in hot melted butter, whisking constantly.
5. Add butter drop by drop at first, then in a thin stream as sauce emulsifies.
6. Whisk in cayenne and salt. Add warm water if too thick.
7. Serve immediately or keep warm over barely simmering water up to 30 minutes.

## Notes

- Temperature is everything: Too hot = scrambled eggs. Too cool = won't emulsify. Keep it gentle.
- Slow with the butter: Add it drop by drop at first. Rushing causes breaking.
- Broken sauce fix: Whisk a fresh yolk in a clean bowl, slowly whisk in the broken sauce.
- Blender method: Blend yolks and lemon juice, then slowly stream in hot butter with blender running.
- Don't hold too long: Best served immediately. It doesn't reheat well.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/hollandaise/

---

# Homemade Bread

Crusty outside, soft inside

Makes: 1 loaf

## Ingredients

- 3 cups all-purpose flour
- 1½ tsp salt
- ½ tsp instant yeast
- 1½ cups warm water

## Steps

1. Mix flour, salt, yeast. Add water, stir until shaggy dough forms.
2. Cover, let rise at room temp 12-18 hours.
3. Turn dough onto floured surface. Fold edges to center, flip seam-side down.
4. Place on parchment-lined baking sheet. Cover, let rise 1-2 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 450°F with Dutch oven inside.
6. Carefully transfer dough to hot Dutch oven. Cover.
7. Bake covered 30 minutes. Uncover, bake 15-20 more until deep golden.
8. Cool completely before slicing.

## Notes

- Long rise develops complex flavor.
- Dutch oven creates steam for crusty crust.
- Cool completely or crumb will be gummy.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/homemade-bread/

---

# Honey Garlic Chicken

Sticky, sweet, savory. The sauce caramelizes into something magical.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 8 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1 Tbsp olive oil
- 8 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup honey
- 1/4 cup soy sauce (or coconut aminos)
- 2 Tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 Tbsp sriracha (optional)
- Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
3. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Place chicken skin-side down. Cook 5-6 minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Flip and cook 2 minutes more. Remove to a plate.
4. Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Whisk in honey, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sriracha (if using). Bring to a simmer.
6. Return chicken to the skillet, skin-side up. Spoon sauce over the chicken.
7. Transfer to oven. Bake 15-18 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F, basting with sauce halfway through.
8. Let rest 5 minutes. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve with rice.

## Notes

- Crispy skin: Don't move the chicken while it's searing. Let it sit undisturbed to crisp.
- Boneless option: Use boneless thighs and reduce oven time to 10-12 minutes.
- The sauce: It thickens as it cools. Add a splash of water when reheating leftovers.
- Make it hotter: Add more sriracha or a few dashes of red pepper flakes.
- Meal prep friendly: Stores well for 4 days. Reheat in the oven to keep skin crispy.

Source: Adapted from Damn Delicious
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/honey-garlic-chicken/

---

# Hot Honey

Sweet heat that goes on everything. The condiment that defined 2025.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 10 min · Makes: 1 cup

## Ingredients

- 1 cup honey
- 2-4 Tbsp hot sauce (like Frank's or Cholula)
- 1/2 tsp crushed red pepper flakes
- 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper (optional, for extra heat)
- 1 Tbsp apple cider vinegar

## Steps

1. Combine honey, hot sauce, red pepper flakes, and cayenne (if using) in a small saucepan.
2. Heat over low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Don't let it boil.
3. Remove from heat and stir in apple cider vinegar.
4. Let cool slightly, then taste and adjust heat level.
5. Transfer to a jar. Store at room temperature up to 1 month.

## Notes

- Heat levels: Start with 2 Tbsp hot sauce and add more to taste. You can always add heat, can't take it away.
- Fresno or serrano: For a fresher kick, slice 1-2 fresh chilis and simmer them in the honey instead of hot sauce.
- Best on: Pizza (especially pepperoni), fried chicken, biscuits, bacon, cheese boards, vanilla ice cream.
- Vinegar: Adds depth and cuts the sweetness. Don't skip it.
- No-cook version: Just whisk ingredients together without heating. Flavor won't meld as well but works in a pinch.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/hot-honey/

---

# Lasagna

The crowd-pleaser. Beef and sausage, three cheeses, feeds an army.

Prep: 45 min · Cook: 2½ hrs · Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- Meat Sauce: 1 lb ground beef, 1 lb Italian sausage, 1 onion diced, 4 garlic cloves minced, 28 oz crushed tomatoes, 12 oz tomato paste, 15 oz tomato sauce, ½ cup water, 2 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp fennel seeds, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, 1 tbsp fresh basil chopped, salt and pepper
- Ricotta Mixture: 30 oz ricotta cheese, 1 egg, ½ cup fresh parsley chopped, ½ tsp salt
- Assembly: 12 lasagna noodles, 1 lb mozzarella sliced (not shredded), 1 cup parmesan grated

## Steps

1. Brown beef and sausage in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Drain fat. Add onion, cook 5 min. Add garlic, cook 1 min.
2. Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, tomato sauce, water, sugar, fennel, Italian seasoning, and basil. Simmer uncovered 1½ hours, stirring occasionally.
3. Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package. Drain and lay flat on oiled sheet to prevent sticking.
4. Mix ricotta with egg, parsley, and salt in a bowl.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F.
6. Layer in 9x13 pan: 1½ cups sauce, 4 noodles, ⅓ ricotta mixture, ⅓ mozzarella slices. Repeat 2 more times. Top with remaining sauce and parmesan.
7. Cover with foil. Bake 25 min. Remove foil, bake 25 min more until bubbly.
8. Rest 15 minutes before cutting (this is crucial for clean slices).

## Notes

- Why 1½ hours for sauce? The long simmer mellows acidity and develops deep flavor. Don't skip it.
- Sliced vs shredded mozzarella: Sliced melts in distinct creamy layers. Shredded clumps.
- Make ahead: Assemble, cover, refrigerate overnight. Add 15 min to bake time if cold.
- Storage: Keeps 5 days refrigerated. Freezes beautifully for 3 months.

Source: Adapted from AllRecipes "World's Best Lasagna" (9.5/10 in taste tests)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/lasagna/

---

# Lemon Bars

Buttery shortbread crust, tangy lemon filling, powdered sugar snow. Perfect.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 45 min · Makes: 16 bars

## Ingredients

**For the crust:**
- 1 cup (2 sticks) butter, softened
- 1/2 cup powdered sugar
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/4 tsp salt
**For the filling:**
- 4 large eggs
- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
- 2/3 cup fresh lemon juice (about 4 lemons)
- Zest of 2 lemons
- Powdered sugar for dusting

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9x13 inch pan with parchment, leaving overhang for easy removal.
2. Make the crust: Beat butter and powdered sugar until fluffy. Add flour and salt, mix until crumbly. Press evenly into pan.
3. Bake crust 18-20 minutes until edges are light golden. Don't overbake—it continues cooking with the filling.
4. Make the filling: Whisk eggs and sugar until smooth. Add flour, lemon juice, and zest. Whisk until combined.
5. Pour filling over hot crust. Return to oven and bake 22-25 minutes until filling is set and no longer jiggles.
6. Cool completely in pan. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
7. Lift out using parchment. Dust generously with powdered sugar. Cut into bars.

## Notes

- Fresh lemons only: Bottled juice doesn't compare. You need that bright, fresh flavor.
- Hot crust: Pour filling onto hot crust to help it set properly.
- Clean cuts: Wipe knife between cuts. Use a thin, sharp blade.
- Storage: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Dust with powdered sugar just before serving.
- Room temp: Let sit 10 minutes before serving for best texture.

Source: Adapted from Sally's Baking Addiction
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/lemon-bars/

---

# Lemon Chicken

Bright, zesty, and weeknight easy. The pan sauce is the star.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 20 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts, pounded to 1/2 inch thick
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1/4 cup flour
- 3 Tbsp olive oil
- 3 Tbsp butter, divided
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup chicken broth
- 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 2 Tbsp capers, drained
- 2 Tbsp fresh parsley, chopped

## Steps

1. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, shaking off excess.
2. Heat olive oil and 1 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
4. Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds.
5. Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice. Scrape up browned bits. Simmer 2 minutes.
6. Stir in remaining butter, lemon zest, and capers. Swirl until butter melts and sauce thickens slightly.
7. Return chicken to pan. Spoon sauce over and cook 1 minute.
8. Top with parsley and serve immediately.

## Notes

- Pound the chicken: Even thickness = even cooking. Use a meat mallet or rolling pin.
- Hot pan: Get the pan hot before adding chicken for proper browning.
- The sauce: Work quickly once you start—it comes together in minutes.
- Serve with: Rice, orzo, roasted potatoes, or crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
- Make it piccata: This is essentially chicken piccata. Add more capers if you love them.

Source: Adapted from RecipeTin Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/lemon-chicken/

---

# Fresh Lemonade

The real thing. No powder, no shortcuts.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 5 min · Makes: 8 cups

_Tested 2026-07. Verified the 1:1:7 juice-sugar-water ratio lands balanced; bottled juice tastes flat, don't._

## Ingredients

- 1 cup fresh lemon juice (about 6 lemons)
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water (for simple syrup)
- 6 cups cold water
- Ice
- Lemon slices for garnish
- Fresh mint (optional)

## Steps

1. Make simple syrup: Heat 1 cup water and sugar in a saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves. Let cool.
2. Juice the lemons. Strain out seeds and pulp (or leave some pulp for texture).
3. Combine simple syrup, lemon juice, and 6 cups cold water in a large pitcher.
4. Taste and adjust: add more water if too sweet, more syrup if too tart.
5. Refrigerate until cold, or serve immediately over lots of ice.
6. Garnish with lemon slices and mint.

## Notes

- Room temperature lemons yield more juice. Roll them on the counter before cutting.
- Simple syrup dissolves better than granulated sugar in cold liquid.
- For pink lemonade: add a splash of cranberry juice or muddle in raspberries.

## When it goes wrong

- Too sour or too sweet → lemons vary wildly; adjust with water or syrup at the end, by taste.
- Gritty sugar at the bottom → skipped the simple syrup; granulated sugar won't dissolve in cold water.
- Bitter aftertaste → juiced too aggressively and got pith oil; squeeze, don't grind.

Source: Classic
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/lemonade/

---

# Baked Mac & Cheese

Creamy, cheesy, golden-crusted perfection.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 35 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 lb elbow macaroni
- 4 tbsp butter
- ¼ cup all-purpose flour
- 3 cups whole milk
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- ¼ tsp cayenne (optional)
- 3 cups sharp cheddar, shredded (12 oz)
- 1 cup Gruyere, shredded (4 oz)
- 1 tsp salt
- ½ tsp black pepper
- Topping: 1 cup panko breadcrumbs, 2 tbsp butter melted, ¼ cup parmesan grated

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Butter a 9x13 baking dish.
2. Cook pasta 2 minutes less than package directions (it'll finish in oven). Drain.
3. Melt butter in large pot over medium heat. Whisk in flour, cook 1 minute (this is the roux).
4. Slowly whisk in milk. Cook, whisking constantly, until thickened, about 5 minutes.
5. Remove from heat. Stir in mustard, cayenne, cheddar, and Gruyere until melted. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Fold in pasta. Transfer to prepared dish.
7. Mix panko with melted butter and parmesan. Sprinkle over top.
8. Bake 25-30 minutes until bubbly. Broil 2 minutes for extra-crispy top (watch carefully).

## Notes

- SHRED YOUR OWN CHEESE. Pre-shredded has anti-caking agents that prevent smooth melting. This is the #1 tip.
- All cheddar works: Skip Gruyere if needed. Use 4 cups sharp cheddar total.
- Stovetop version: Skip baking. Just serve the cheese sauce with pasta directly.
- Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Reheat with splash of milk to restore creaminess.

Source: Adapted from RecipeTin Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/mac-and-cheese/

---

# Macaroni Salad

Creamy, tangy, picnic classic

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 lb elbow macaroni
- ¾ cup mayonnaise
- 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar
- 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
- 2 celery ribs, diced
- ¼ cup red onion, minced
- ¼ cup dill pickles, diced
- 2 tbsp parsley
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cook pasta until tender. Rinse with cold water.
2. Whisk mayo, vinegar, mustard.
3. Add pasta, celery, onion, pickles, parsley. Toss.
4. Season generously.
5. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
6. Before serving, stir and adjust. Add more mayo if dry.

## Notes

- Pasta absorbs dressing - make it wetter than you think.
- Tastes better next day.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/macaroni-salad/

---

# Marinara Sauce

Marcella Hazan's legendary 3-ingredient sauce. Simplicity perfected.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 45 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 28 oz whole San Marzano tomatoes
- 5 tbsp butter
- 1 medium onion, peeled and halved
- Salt to taste

## Steps

1. Put tomatoes (with their juice), butter, and onion halves in a saucepan.
2. Add a pinch of salt.
3. Bring to a simmer over medium heat. Reduce heat to maintain a gentle, steady simmer.
4. Cook uncovered for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally and crushing tomatoes with a wooden spoon.
5. Remove and discard the onion.
6. Taste and add more salt if needed.
7. Toss with hot pasta. Serve with fresh parmesan.

## Notes

- BUTTER not olive oil: This is the whole point. Butter makes it silky and rich. Trust Marcella.
- Onion stays whole: It flavors the sauce subtly without overpowering. Discard at the end.
- Don't add garlic: Sounds wrong, but this sauce doesn't need it. The simplicity IS the flavor.
- San Marzano tomatoes: Worth the extra dollar. Sweeter, less acidic.
- Storage: Keeps 1 week refrigerated, freezes well for 3 months.

Source: Marcella Hazan from Essentials of Classic Italian Cooking
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/marinara-sauce/

---

# Marry Me Chicken

Creamy sun-dried tomato sauce so good it allegedly inspires proposals.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 30 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tsp Italian seasoning
- 1/2 tsp paprika
- 3 Tbsp olive oil, divided
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes, drained and chopped
- 1 cup chicken broth
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 1/2 cup parmesan cheese, grated
- 1/4 cup fresh basil, chopped
- 1 tsp red pepper flakes (optional)

## Steps

1. Pound chicken breasts to even thickness (about 3/4 inch). Season both sides with salt, pepper, Italian seasoning, and paprika.
2. Heat 2 Tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken 5-6 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Remove and set aside.
3. Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining olive oil, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes. Sauté 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
4. Pour in chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Let simmer 2 minutes.
5. Stir in heavy cream and parmesan. Simmer 3-4 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
6. Return chicken to the skillet. Spoon sauce over chicken and simmer 2-3 minutes to let flavors meld.
7. Top with fresh basil and red pepper flakes if using. Serve immediately.

## Notes

- The sauce: Don't skip scraping the browned bits—that's where the flavor lives.
- Sun-dried tomatoes: Use oil-packed for more flavor. Drain well before chopping.
- Serve with: Pasta, crusty bread, rice, or mashed potatoes to soak up the sauce.
- Make it lighter: Use half-and-half instead of heavy cream.
- Storage: Refrigerate up to 3 days. Reheat gently to avoid breaking the sauce.

Source: Adapted from RecipeTin Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/marry-me-chicken/

---

# Mashed Potatoes

Creamy, buttery, never gluey. One crucial tip makes the difference.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 4 lbs Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cut into 1" chunks
- 1½ sticks butter (12 tbsp)
- 1½ cups half-and-half (or 1 cup milk + ½ cup cream)
- 2 tsp salt, plus more for cooking water
- Black pepper to taste

## Steps

1. Place potatoes in large pot. Cover with cold water by 1 inch. Add 1 tablespoon salt.
2. Bring to boil over high heat. Reduce to medium, simmer until fork-tender, 20-25 minutes.
3. While potatoes cook, warm butter and half-and-half in small saucepan until butter melts. Keep warm.
4. Drain potatoes. Return to pot over low heat for 1 minute to dry out any excess moisture.
5. For smooth: Pass through ricer or food mill into large bowl. For chunky: Mash with potato masher.
6. Add BUTTER FIRST. Fold in until absorbed. This coats the starch and prevents glueyness.
7. Then fold in warm half-and-half gradually until desired consistency.
8. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

## Notes

- BUTTER FIRST: This is the secret. Fat coats starch granules, preventing gluey texture. Don't add milk first.
- Yukon Gold: Naturally buttery flavor and creamy texture. Russets work but are starchier.
- Use a ricer: Creates smooth potatoes without overworking. Mashing can make them gluey.
- Warm the dairy: Cold liquid cools potatoes and makes them gummy. Always warm first.
- Make ahead: Hold in slow cooker on warm up to 2 hours. Add splash of cream when serving.

Source: Adapted from America's Test Kitchen
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/mashed-potatoes/

---

# Meatloaf

Moist inside, caramelized outside. The sheet pan method.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 1 hr · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- Meatloaf: 2 lbs ground beef (80/20), 1 small onion grated (not diced), 3 garlic cloves minced, ¾ cup panko breadcrumbs, ½ cup milk, 2 eggs, 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper, 1 tsp Italian seasoning
- Glaze: ½ cup ketchup, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a rimmed baking sheet with foil and set a wire rack on top (or just use foil).
2. Mix panko and milk in large bowl. Let sit 5 minutes (this is the panade).
3. Add beef, grated onion, garlic, eggs, Worcestershire, salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning. Mix gently with hands until just combined. Don't overwork.
4. Shape into a free-form loaf on prepared pan, about 9x5 inches.
5. Bake 40 minutes.
6. While baking, mix glaze ingredients.
7. Remove from oven, brush with half the glaze. Increase oven to 400°F.
8. Bake 15-20 more minutes until internal temp reaches 160°F. Brush with remaining glaze halfway through.
9. Rest 10 minutes before slicing.

## Notes

- GRATE the onion: Releases moisture without chunks. Keeps meatloaf incredibly moist.
- Free-form on sheet pan: Fat drains away, all sides get browned. Loaf pans trap grease and steam the meat.
- The panade: Milk-soaked breadcrumbs keep it tender. Same technique as meatballs.
- Italian sausage variation: Use 1 lb beef + 1 lb Italian sausage for more flavor.
- Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Makes excellent sandwiches cold.

Source: Adapted from RecipeTin Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/meatloaf/

---

# Minestrone

Hearty Italian vegetable soup

Serves: 8-10

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 oz pancetta, diced
- 2 onions, diced
- 2 celery ribs, diced
- 1 carrot, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 zucchini, diced
- 1 (14.5 oz) can diced tomatoes
- 8 cups chicken broth
- 1 Parmesan rind (optional)
- 1 (15 oz) can cannellini beans
- 1 cup small pasta (ditalini)
- 2 cups chopped kale
- ¼ cup fresh basil

## Steps

1. Cook pancetta in oil until rendered, 3-4 minutes.
2. Add onions, celery, carrot. Cook 8 minutes until softened.
3. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add zucchini, cook 2 minutes.
4. Add tomatoes, broth, Parmesan rind. Simmer 30 minutes.
5. Add beans and pasta. Cook until pasta is al dente, 10 minutes.
6. Add kale, cook 2 minutes. Remove rind, stir in basil. Season to taste.

## Notes

- Parmesan rind adds incredible umami depth.
- Add pasta near end so it doesn't get mushy.
- Even better the next day.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/minestrone/

---

# French Omelette

Silky eggs, pale yellow, classic technique

Serves: 1

## Ingredients

- 3 large eggs
- 1 tbsp butter
- Salt and pepper
- Optional: cheese, herbs, ham

## Steps

1. Beat eggs with fork until just combined. Season.
2. Heat 8-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high. Add butter.
3. When foam subsides, add eggs.
4. Stir eggs with fork while shaking pan. Small curds form.
5. When mostly set but still wet on top, stop stirring.
6. Add fillings if using.
7. Tilt pan and roll omelette onto plate.
8. Should be pale yellow outside, creamy inside.

## Notes

- Practice makes perfect.
- Don't let it brown.
- Entire process takes under 2 minutes.

Source: Jacques Pépin / Serious Eats (Jacques Pépin classic)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/omelette/

---

# Overnight Oats

Prep tonight, grab and go tomorrow. Breakfast that makes itself.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 0 min (8 hours soak) · Serves: 1

## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup old-fashioned rolled oats
- 1/2 cup milk (any kind)
- 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
- 1 Tbsp chia seeds (optional)
- 1 Tbsp maple syrup or honey
- 1/4 tsp vanilla extract
- Pinch of salt
- Toppings: fresh fruit, nuts, nut butter, granola

## Steps

1. Combine oats, milk, yogurt, chia seeds, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt in a jar or container.
2. Stir well to combine. Make sure all oats are submerged.
3. Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
4. In the morning, stir. Add more milk if too thick.
5. Top with fresh fruit, nuts, or other toppings and eat cold, or microwave 1-2 minutes if you prefer it warm.

## Notes

- Oat type: Use old-fashioned rolled oats. Quick oats get too mushy. Steel cut won't soften enough.
- Ratio: The 1:1 ratio of oats to liquid is key. Adjust to your preferred thickness.
- Chia seeds: Not essential but add protein and help thicken. Flax works too.
- Meal prep: Make 5 jars on Sunday. They last up to 5 days in the fridge.
- Flavor combos: PB&J (peanut butter + jam), Banana Nut (banana + walnuts + cinnamon), Berry Blast (mixed berries + honey), Apple Pie (diced apple + cinnamon + pecans).

Source: Adapted from Cookie and Kate
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/overnight-oats/

---

# Pan-Seared Salmon

Crispy skin, perfectly cooked center

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 salmon fillets (6 oz each, skin-on)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 lemon, juiced
- 2 tbsp fresh dill or parsley
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Pat salmon very dry. Season flesh side with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in nonstick skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
3. Place salmon skin-side down. Press firmly 10 seconds.
4. Cook without moving until skin is crispy and salmon is cooked ⅔ up, 5-6 minutes.
5. Flip. Add butter and garlic. Cook 1-2 minutes, basting.
6. Remove. Drizzle with lemon juice and herbs.
7. Serve skin-side up to keep skin crispy.

## Notes

- Dry skin = crispy skin.
- Don't move the fish.
- Medium-rare center (125°F) is ideal.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pan-seared-salmon/

---

# Pan-Seared Steak

Restaurant-quality crust, butter-basted

Serves: 2

## Ingredients

- 2 ribeye or NY strip steaks (1-1.5 inches)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 tbsp butter
- 4 cloves garlic, smashed
- 4 sprigs thyme
- Flaky salt and coarse pepper

## Steps

1. Remove steaks from fridge 30-60 minutes before. Pat very dry. Season generously.
2. Heat oil in cast iron over high until smoking.
3. Lay steaks in pan. Sear 3-4 minutes until deep crust forms.
4. Flip. Add butter, garlic, herbs. Tilt pan and baste continuously 2-3 minutes.
5. Check temp: 120°F rare, 130°F medium-rare, 140°F medium.
6. Rest 5-10 minutes. Slice against grain.

## Notes

- Room temp steak cooks evenly.
- Dry surface = better crust.
- Don't touch while searing.
- Resting redistributes juices.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pan-seared-steak/

---

# Buttermilk Pancakes

Light, fluffy, tangy. Rest the batter—trust the process.

Prep: 10 min · Makes: 12

## Ingredients

- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1 tsp baking soda
- ½ tsp salt
- 2 cups buttermilk
- ¼ cup sour cream
- 2 eggs
- 3 tbsp butter, melted
- 1 tsp vanilla extract

## Steps

1. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
2. In another bowl, whisk buttermilk, sour cream, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla.
3. Pour wet ingredients into dry. Stir until JUST combined. Lumps are okay—don't overmix or you'll get tough pancakes.
4. Let batter rest 10 minutes. This allows gluten to relax and leavening to activate.
5. Heat griddle or nonstick pan over medium-low heat. Lightly butter the surface.
6. Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on surface AND edges look set, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Flip when holes form in the bubbles (not just bubbles appearing). Cook 1-2 minutes more until golden.
8. Serve immediately with butter and maple syrup.

## Notes

- REAL buttermilk matters: The acid reacts with baking soda for lift. Homemade substitute works but won't be as fluffy.
- DON'T overmix: Lumpy batter = fluffy pancakes. Smooth batter = tough pancakes.
- REST the batter: 10 minutes lets the flour hydrate and gluten relax.
- When to flip: Not just when bubbles appear—wait until holes form and edges are set.
- Keep warm: Hold cooked pancakes on wire rack in 200°F oven while cooking the rest.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pancakes/

---

# Pasta Primavera

Fresh vegetables in light cream sauce

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb penne
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 zucchini, sliced
- 1 yellow squash, sliced
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1 cup asparagus, cut up
- 1 cup frozen peas
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- ½ cup white wine
- ½ cup heavy cream
- 1 cup Parmesan
- ¼ cup basil, torn

## Steps

1. Cook pasta. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
2. Sauté zucchini and squash in oil until browned, 3-4 minutes. Set aside.
3. Add butter, sauté asparagus 2 minutes. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
4. Add wine, reduce by half. Add cream and tomatoes.
5. Return vegetables with peas. Simmer 2 minutes.
6. Add pasta and Parmesan. Toss, adding pasta water for consistency.
7. Stir in basil, season to taste.

## Notes

- Cook each vegetable separately for best doneness.
- Use whatever vegetables are in season.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pasta-primavera/

---

# Italian Pasta Salad

The potluck MVP. Better the next day.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- 1 lb rotini or fusilli pasta
- 1 cup Italian dressing
- 4 oz salami, diced
- 4 oz pepperoni, halved
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1 cup black olives, sliced
- 1 cup mozzarella pearls (or diced mozzarella)
- 1/2 red onion, thinly sliced
- 1/2 cup pepperoncini, sliced
- 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
- 1/2 cup parmesan, shaved
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

1. Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking.
2. Toss pasta with half the Italian dressing while still slightly warm.
3. Add salami, pepperoni, tomatoes, olives, mozzarella, red onion, and pepperoncini.
4. Pour remaining dressing over salad. Toss to combine.
5. Add parsley and parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving. Toss again and add more dressing if needed.

## Notes

- Rinse the pasta: Cold water stops cooking and removes excess starch so it doesn't clump.
- Dress while warm: Pasta absorbs dressing better when slightly warm.
- Homemade dressing: Whisk 1/2 cup olive oil, 1/4 cup red wine vinegar, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, 1 clove minced garlic, salt and pepper.
- Make ahead: Keeps 5 days refrigerated. Add more dressing before serving—pasta absorbs it.
- Vegetarian: Skip the meat, add artichoke hearts and roasted red peppers.

Source: Adapted from Natasha's Kitchen
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pasta-salad/

---

# Peach Cobbler

Juicy fruit, biscuit topping

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- Filling: 3 lbs ripe peaches sliced, ½ cup sugar, 1 tbsp cornstarch, 1 tsp vanilla, ¼ tsp almond extract
- Topping: 1½ cups flour, ⅓ cup sugar + extra, 1½ tsp baking powder, ¼ tsp salt, 6 tbsp cold butter cubed, ½ cup heavy cream

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Toss peaches with sugar, cornstarch, extracts. Pour into 9x13 dish.
3. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, salt. Cut in butter until pea-sized.
4. Add cream, stir until just combined.
5. Drop biscuit dough over filling in mounds. Sprinkle with sugar.
6. Bake until golden and bubbling, 45-55 minutes.
7. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream.

## Notes

- Ripe peaches are essential.
- Don't spread dough - leave it chunky.

Source: Serious Eats (Stella Parks tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/peach-cobbler/

---

# Peanut Butter Cookies

Three ingredients. No flour. Impossibly good.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 10 min · Makes: 24 cookies

## Ingredients

- 1 cup peanut butter (creamy or crunchy)
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 large egg
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract (optional)
- 1/2 tsp baking soda (optional, for puffier cookies)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line baking sheets with parchment.
2. Mix peanut butter, sugar, and egg until smooth and well combined.
3. Add vanilla and baking soda if using.
4. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Place on baking sheets.
5. Press each ball with a fork in a crosshatch pattern, flattening slightly.
6. Bake 8-10 minutes until edges are just set. Centers will look underdone.
7. Cool on pan 5 minutes—they firm up as they cool. Transfer to wire rack.

## Notes

- Peanut butter: Use regular peanut butter, not natural. Natural separates and changes the texture.
- The fork marks: Traditional and functional—they help cookies bake evenly.
- Don't overbake: Pull them when they look slightly underdone. They continue cooking on the pan.
- Gluten-free: These are naturally gluten-free as written.
- Add-ins: Press a Hershey's Kiss into each cookie right after baking for peanut butter blossoms.

Source: Adapted from Allrecipes
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/peanut-butter-cookies/

---

# Penne alla Vodka

Creamy tomato sauce with a kick. The vodka cooks off but the magic stays.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb penne pasta
- 2 Tbsp olive oil
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
- 1 can (28 oz) crushed San Marzano tomatoes
- 1/2 cup vodka
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1/2 cup parmesan cheese, grated
- Fresh basil for serving

## Steps

1. Cook penne in salted water until just shy of al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
2. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes. Sauté 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Add crushed tomatoes and salt. Simmer 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove pan from heat. Add vodka, then return to heat. Simmer 7-8 minutes until sauce reduces and vodka smell dissipates.
5. Reduce heat to low. Stir in heavy cream and simmer 2-3 minutes until sauce turns pink and thickens slightly.
6. Add drained pasta and parmesan. Toss to coat, adding pasta water as needed for consistency.
7. Serve topped with fresh basil and extra parmesan.

## Notes

- Why vodka: It releases flavor compounds in the tomatoes that aren't alcohol or water soluble. The alcohol cooks off.
- Flambé warning: Add vodka off heat to prevent flare-ups, then return to the burner.
- San Marzano: These tomatoes are sweeter and less acidic. Worth seeking out.
- Make it richer: Add 2 Tbsp butter at the end for extra silkiness.
- Add protein: Grilled chicken, Italian sausage, or pancetta all work beautifully.

Source: Adapted from NYT Cooking
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/penne-alla-vodka/

---

# Basil Pesto

Fresh, bright, and takes 5 minutes. Once you make it yourself, you won't go back.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Makes: 1 cup

## Ingredients

- 2 cups fresh basil leaves, packed
- 1/3 cup pine nuts (or walnuts)
- 3 cloves garlic
- 1/2 cup parmesan cheese, grated
- 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1 Tbsp lemon juice (optional, keeps it green)

## Steps

1. Toast pine nuts in a dry skillet over medium heat 2-3 minutes until golden. Watch carefully—they burn fast.
2. Combine basil, toasted pine nuts, and garlic in a food processor. Pulse until coarsely chopped.
3. Add parmesan, salt, and pepper. Pulse to combine.
4. With the processor running, slowly drizzle in olive oil until smooth.
5. Taste and adjust seasoning. Add lemon juice if using.
6. Use immediately or store with a thin layer of olive oil on top to prevent browning.

## Notes

- Pine nut swap: Walnuts, almonds, or cashews work. Pine nuts are traditional but expensive.
- Keeping it green: A squeeze of lemon and blanching the basil (10 seconds in boiling water, then ice bath) help prevent browning.
- Storage: Refrigerate up to 1 week with oil on top. Freeze in ice cube trays up to 3 months.
- Pasta ratio: Use about 1/4 cup pesto per serving of pasta, loosened with pasta water.
- Beyond pasta: Spread on sandwiches, mix into mayo, drizzle on pizza, stir into soups, or use as a marinade.

Source: Adapted from Bon Appétit
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pesto/

---

# Philly Cheesesteak

Thinly sliced beef, gooey cheese, soft roll

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs ribeye steak
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 large onion, sliced thin
- 8 slices American cheese (or Cheez Whiz)
- 4 hoagie rolls
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Freeze ribeye 30-60 minutes until firm. Slice as thin as possible against grain.
2. Cook onions in 1 tbsp oil until caramelized, 10-15 minutes. Season, transfer out.
3. Increase heat to high. Add remaining oil.
4. Spread steak thin, season. Sear 30 seconds, chop and stir. Cook until just done.
5. Pile into 4 portions. Top with cheese. Cover to melt.
6. Scoop into rolls with onions.

## Notes

- Freezing makes thin slicing possible.
- American cheese melts perfectly.
- Don't overcook - should be tender.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/philly-cheesesteak/

---

# Pie Crust

Flaky, buttery, foolproof

Makes: 2 crusts

## Ingredients

- 2½ cups flour
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) cold butter, cubed
- ½ cup ice water

## Steps

1. Pulse flour, sugar, salt in food processor.
2. Add butter, pulse until pea-sized pieces remain.
3. Drizzle in ice water, pulse until dough just comes together.
4. Divide in half, flatten into disks.
5. Wrap in plastic, refrigerate at least 1 hour.
6. Roll on floured surface to 12-inch circle.
7. Fit into pie plate, trim, crimp edges.
8. Chill before filling.

## Notes

- Keep everything cold.
- Don't overwork the dough.
- Visible butter pieces = flaky crust.

Source: Serious Eats / Stella Parks (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pie-crust/

---

# Pizza Dough

Chewy, crispy, restaurant quality

Makes: 2 pizzas

## Ingredients

- 3 cups bread flour
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 2¼ tsp instant yeast
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup warm water
- 2 tbsp olive oil

## Steps

1. Mix flour, sugar, yeast, salt.
2. Add water and oil. Mix until dough forms.
3. Knead until smooth and elastic, 8-10 minutes.
4. Place in oiled bowl. Cover, let rise until doubled, 1-2 hours.
5. Punch down. Divide in half.
6. Use immediately or refrigerate up to 3 days.
7. Let cold dough come to room temp before shaping.

## Notes

- Bread flour has more gluten for chewy texture.
- Cold rise develops more flavor.
- High heat is essential for crispy crust.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pizza-dough/

---

# Pan-Seared Pork Chops

Golden crust, juicy center, butter-basted

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 bone-in pork chops (1-inch thick)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 tbsp butter
- 4 cloves garlic, smashed
- 4 sprigs fresh thyme
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Remove chops from fridge 30 minutes before. Pat very dry.
2. Season generously with salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in cast iron over medium-high until smoking.
4. Sear without moving 4 minutes until deep golden.
5. Flip. Add butter, garlic, thyme.
6. Tilt pan and baste 3-4 minutes.
7. Check temp: 140°F for slightly pink, 145°F for medium.
8. Rest 5 minutes.

## Notes

- Thick-cut bone-in stays juicier.
- Pork is safe at 145°F.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pork-chops/

---

# Pork Fried Rice

Classic Chinese takeout, homemade

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 4 cups day-old white rice, cold
- ½ lb pork tenderloin or char siu, diced
- 3 tbsp vegetable oil
- 3 eggs, beaten
- 1 cup frozen peas
- 4 scallions, sliced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- White pepper

## Steps

1. Heat wok until smoking. Add 1 tbsp oil. Stir-fry pork until cooked, 2-3 minutes. Transfer out.
2. Add 1 tbsp oil. Scramble eggs, break into pieces. Transfer out.
3. Add remaining oil. Stir-fry garlic 30 seconds.
4. Add rice, press flat. Let sit 30 seconds, then stir. Repeat until hot and crispy.
5. Add peas, cook 1 minute. Return pork and eggs. Add soy sauce, toss.
6. Drizzle with sesame oil, add scallions and white pepper.

## Notes

- Char siu is traditional.
- Day-old rice is essential.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pork-fried-rice/

---

# Roasted Pork Tenderloin

Quick-roasting, herb-crusted, juicy

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 2 pork tenderloins (about 2 lbs)
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tsp sugar
- 1 tbsp fresh rosemary, minced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- ½ tsp red pepper flakes
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Pat tenderloins dry.
2. Mix sugar, rosemary, garlic, pepper flakes, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp pepper.
3. Rub with oil, then coat with spice mixture.
4. Sear in oven-safe skillet over medium-high on all sides, 8 minutes.
5. Transfer to 400°F oven. Roast until 140-145°F, 15-20 minutes.
6. Rest 10 minutes. Slice into medallions.

## Notes

- Tenderloins are lean - don't overcook.
- Sugar helps browning.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pork-tenderloin/

---

# Pot Roast

Fork-tender beef, rich gravy

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 1 boneless beef chuck roast (3-4 lbs)
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 large onion, sliced
- 2 carrots, chunked
- 2 celery ribs, chunked
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup red wine
- 2 cups beef broth
- 1 (14 oz) can diced tomatoes
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 sprigs thyme

## Steps

1. Season roast generously. Brown in oil on all sides, 10-12 minutes. Transfer out.
2. Cook onion, carrots, celery until soft, 8 minutes. Add garlic, 30 seconds.
3. Add wine, scrape up bits. Reduce by half.
4. Add broth, tomatoes, bay leaves, thyme. Return roast.
5. Cover, braise at 300°F until fork-tender, 3-3.5 hours.
6. Slice against grain. Serve with strained sauce and vegetables.

## Notes

- Chuck has best fat marbling.
- Low and slow is essential.
- Let meat rest before slicing.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pot-roast/

---

# Potato Salad

Creamy, classic American style

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 3 lbs Yukon Gold potatoes
- 2 tbsp white vinegar
- ¾ cup mayonnaise
- 2 tbsp yellow mustard
- 3 celery ribs, diced
- ¼ cup red onion, minced
- 3 hard-boiled eggs, chopped
- 2 tbsp fresh dill or parsley
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cover potatoes with salted water. Boil until fork-tender, 15-20 minutes.
2. Drain. When cool enough, cut into ¾-inch cubes.
3. Toss warm potatoes with vinegar and ½ tsp salt.
4. Let cool to room temperature.
5. Mix mayo and mustard. Add to potatoes with celery, onion, eggs, herbs.
6. Fold gently. Season.
7. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.

## Notes

- Dress potatoes while warm - absorbs more flavor.
- Yukon Golds hold shape better.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/potato-salad/

---

# Loaded Baked Potato Soup

All the toppings, in soup form

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 8 slices bacon, chopped
- 2½ lbs russet potatoes
- 1 medium onion, chopped
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 tbsp flour
- 5 cups chicken broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 sprig thyme
- 4 oz sharp cheddar, shredded
- ½ cup sour cream
- 4 scallions, sliced
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Cook bacon until crisp. Reserve for garnish. Peel potatoes, save peels, cube potatoes.
2. Fry potato skins in bacon fat until crisp, about 8 minutes. Reserve.
3. Cook onion in fat until golden, 6 minutes. Add garlic and flour, cook 1 minute.
4. Whisk in broth and cream. Add thyme and potatoes. Simmer until tender, 20 minutes.
5. Remove 2 cups potatoes. Purée rest until smooth.
6. Off heat, stir in cheese until melted, then sour cream. Return potato chunks.
7. Top with bacon, fried skins, scallions, extra cheese and sour cream.

## Notes

- Don't skip frying potato skins - incredible texture.
- Add sour cream off heat to prevent curdling.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/potato-soup/

---

# Pound Cake

Dense, buttery, versatile

Serves: 12

## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter, softened
- 3 cups sugar
- 6 eggs
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 tsp vanilla
- ¼ tsp salt

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease and flour bundt pan.
2. Beat butter and sugar until very light and fluffy, 5 minutes.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each.
4. Combine flour and salt. Add alternately with sour cream, starting and ending with flour.
5. Stir in vanilla.
6. Pour into pan. Bake 60-75 minutes until toothpick comes out clean.
7. Cool in pan 15 minutes, then turn out.

## Notes

- Creaming butter and sugar is the key to texture.
- Don't open oven door for first 45 minutes.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pound-cake/

---

# Protein Ice Cream

Creami-style pint with protein math that actually checks out.

Prep: 5 min (plus 24 hr freeze) · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 2

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — With a Creami and pudding mix it's legitimately creamy; the no-machine fallback is decent soft-serve you have to eat on the spot.

_Tested 2026-07. Old version claimed 25g protein per serving from one scoop split across two servings (about 13g); rebuilt as a two-scoop Creami pint with a freezer-only fallback._

## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups (360ml) milk of choice (2% or whole = creamier)
- 2 scoops (60-65g) whey or whey-casein blend protein powder (about 50g protein total)
- 1 Tbsp sugar-free instant vanilla pudding mix (the texture secret)
- 1 Tbsp maple syrup or honey (or 2 tsp allulose)
- Pinch of fine salt
- Optional mix-ins: chocolate chips, crushed cookies, peanut butter

## Steps

1. Whisk milk, protein powder, pudding mix, sweetener, and salt until completely smooth (or blend 10 seconds - no longer, or you'll whip in foam).
2. Pour into a Creami pint. Do not pass the max fill line. Freeze on a flat surface for 24 hours.
3. Spin on the Lite Ice Cream setting. It will come out crumbly and chalky-looking. That's normal.
4. Add 1 Tbsp milk and hit Re-spin. Now it's ice cream.
5. For mix-ins: dig a hole to the bottom, drop them in, run the Mix-in cycle.
6. No machine? Pour the base into an ice cube tray, freeze solid, then blitz the cubes in a food processor with a splash of milk until it looks like soft-serve. Eat it immediately - it doesn't re-freeze well.

## Notes

- The math: one 30-32g scoop of whey has 24-25g protein. Two scoops per pint = about 50g, so half a pint = 25g. The old one-scoop-split-two-ways version was a 13g serving pretending to be 25.
- Pudding mix isn't optional if you want creamy - its modified starch blocks the ice crystals that make protein bases icy.
- Whey-casein blends spin creamier than pure whey isolate. Plant proteins work but come out denser.

## When it goes wrong

- Crumbly and chalky after the first spin → normal for high-protein bases; add 1 Tbsp milk and re-spin.
- Icy instead of creamy → you skipped the pudding mix or used watery milk; the starch is what blocks ice crystals.
- Frozen pint has a hollow, foamy top → over-blended base; whisk gently and let any foam settle before freezing.
- Freezer-fallback batch sets like a brick → that's what protein and water do; re-blitz with a splash of milk instead of trying to scoop it.

Source: Fitness community / Ninja Creami method
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/protein-ice-cream/

---

# Pulled Pork

Fall-apart tender, smoky, saucy

Serves: 10-12

## Ingredients

- 1 bone-in pork shoulder (6-8 lbs)
- 2 tbsp brown sugar
- 2 tbsp paprika
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tbsp cumin
- 1 tbsp garlic powder
- 1 tbsp onion powder
- 2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tbsp salt
- 1 cup apple cider vinegar
- BBQ sauce

## Steps

1. Mix all spices for dry rub. Apply generously all over pork.
2. Place fat-side up in roasting pan. Pour vinegar in bottom.
3. Cover tightly with foil. Roast at 300°F for 8-10 hours, until 195-205°F and meat shreds easily.
4. Rest 30 minutes.
5. Shred meat, discarding fat and bone. Toss with pan juices and BBQ sauce.
6. Serve on soft buns with coleslaw.

## Notes

- Bone-in shoulder has best flavor.
- Low and slow breaks down collagen.
- Mix crispy bark into pulled meat.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pulled-pork/

---

# Pumpkin Pie

Spiced, custardy, Thanksgiving essential

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 (9-inch) pie crust, blind-baked
- 1 (15 oz) can pumpkin puree
- ¾ cup brown sugar
- 1 tsp cinnamon
- ½ tsp ginger
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- ¼ tsp cloves
- ½ tsp salt
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup heavy cream
- Whipped cream

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Cook pumpkin in saucepan over medium, stirring, until thickened, 5-8 minutes.
3. Whisk in sugar, spices, salt. Cool slightly.
4. Whisk in eggs and cream.
5. Pour into crust. Bake 15 minutes.
6. Reduce to 350°F. Bake 40-50 more until center is just set.
7. Cool to room temperature. Chill.
8. Serve with whipped cream.

## Notes

- Cooking pumpkin first intensifies flavor.
- Center should wobble when done.

Source: Serious Eats / Stella Parks (BraveTart tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/pumpkin-pie/

---

# Quiche Lorraine

Bacon, cheese, custardy filling

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 1 (9-inch) pie crust, blind-baked
- 6 slices bacon, chopped
- 1 cup Gruyère, shredded
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 cup whole milk
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cook bacon until crisp. Drain.
3. Scatter bacon and cheese in pie crust.
4. Whisk eggs, cream, milk, nutmeg, ½ tsp salt, ¼ tsp pepper.
5. Pour custard into crust.
6. Bake until center is just set and jiggles slightly, 45-50 minutes.
7. Cool 15 minutes before slicing.

## Notes

- Blind-baking prevents soggy crust.
- Custard should jiggle like jello when done.
- Don't overbake.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/quiche/

---

# Ranch Dressing

Homemade ranch destroys bottled. Creamy, herby, and ready in 5 minutes.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Makes: 1.5 cups

## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup mayonnaise
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 Tbsp fresh chives, minced
- 1 Tbsp fresh dill, minced (or 1 tsp dried)
- 1 Tbsp fresh parsley, minced
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1/2 tsp onion powder
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1 tsp lemon juice

## Steps

1. Whisk together mayonnaise, sour cream, and buttermilk until smooth.
2. Add chives, dill, parsley, garlic, onion powder, salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
3. Whisk until combined. Taste and adjust seasoning.
4. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving to let flavors meld.
5. Thin with more buttermilk if needed for dressing consistency. Keep thicker for dipping.

## Notes
