- No buttermilk: Mix 1/2 cup milk with 1/2 Tbsp lemon juice. Let sit 5 minutes.
- Dried herbs: Use 1 tsp each dried chives, dill, and parsley if fresh aren't available.
- Make it a dip: Use less buttermilk for thicker consistency.
- Storage: Refrigerate up to 1 week.
- Greek yogurt: Substitute for sour cream for a lighter version.

Source: Adapted from The Pioneer Woman
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/ranch-dressing/

---

# Rice Krispie Treats

Three ingredients. Ten minutes. Pure nostalgic bliss.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 5 min · Makes: 12 squares

## Ingredients

- 3 Tbsp butter
- 1 package (10 oz) marshmallows (about 40 regular)
- 6 cups Rice Krispies cereal

## Steps

1. Grease a 9x13 inch pan with butter or cooking spray.
2. Melt butter in a large pot over low heat.
3. Add marshmallows and stir constantly until completely melted and smooth.
4. Remove from heat. Immediately add Rice Krispies and stir until well coated.
5. Press mixture into prepared pan using a greased spatula or wax paper.
6. Let cool completely before cutting into squares.

## Notes

- Low heat: High heat makes marshmallows tough. Keep it low and stir constantly.
- Fresh marshmallows: Stale marshmallows don't melt smoothly.
- Don't press too hard: Light pressing keeps them chewy. Compacted treats are dense.
- Variations: Add 1/2 cup chocolate chips, sprinkles, or peanut butter to the marshmallow mixture.
- Storage: Cover tightly at room temperature up to 2 days. Don't refrigerate—they get hard.

Source: Adapted from Kellogg's
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/rice-krispie-treats/

---

# Rice Pilaf

Buttery, toasted, fluffy

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 1½ cups long-grain white rice
- 2¼ cups chicken broth
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 small onion, minced
- ½ tsp salt

## Steps

1. Rinse rice until water runs clear. Drain.
2. Melt butter over medium. Cook onion 3 minutes.
3. Add rice. Stir to coat. Toast until edges turn translucent, 2-3 minutes.
4. Add broth and salt. Bring to boil.
5. Cover, reduce to low. Simmer 18 minutes.
6. Remove from heat. Let sit 5 minutes covered.
7. Fluff with fork.

## Notes

- Rinsing removes starch for fluffy rice.
- Toasting adds nutty flavor.
- Don't lift lid while cooking.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/rice-pilaf/

---

# Roast Chicken

The Thomas Keller method. Three ingredients. Perfect every time.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 1 hr · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 whole chicken (3-4 lbs)
- Kosher salt
- Black pepper

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Let chicken sit at room temperature 30 minutes while oven heats.
2. Remove chicken from packaging. Remove giblets from cavity. Pat VERY dry inside and out with paper towels. This is crucial for crispy skin.
3. Truss the legs: tie them together with kitchen twine. Tuck wing tips under the body.
4. Season generously with salt and pepper from about 1 foot above the bird (for even coverage). Season inside the cavity too.
5. Place chicken breast-side up in cast iron skillet or roasting pan. No rack needed.
6. Roast 50-60 minutes. DO NOT BASTE. Do not open the oven door. Basting creates steam and prevents crispy skin.
7. Chicken is done when thigh reaches 165°F (or when juices run clear when thigh is pierced).
8. Rest 15 minutes before carving. The juices need to redistribute.

## Notes

- DRY = CRISPY: The #1 secret. Wet skin steams instead of crisping. Pat it bone dry.
- No basting: Sounds wrong, but every time you open the oven you lose heat and add moisture. Trust the method.
- High heat the whole time: This is what makes the skin shatteringly crispy.
- Use the drippings: Spoon them over carved meat, or make a quick pan sauce.
- Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Shred leftover meat for sandwiches, soup, or tacos.

Source: Thomas Keller's method from Bouchon
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/roast-chicken/

---

# Roasted Vegetables

Caramelized, tender, versatile

Serves: 4-6

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs mixed vegetables (carrots, broccoli, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts)
- 3 tbsp olive oil
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp fresh thyme or rosemary
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Cut vegetables into similar sizes.
3. Toss with oil, garlic, herbs, salt, pepper.
4. Spread in single layer on baking sheet. Don't crowd.
5. Roast 20 minutes. Stir.
6. Continue roasting until browned and tender, 15-25 more minutes.

## Notes

- Don't crowd the pan.
- Use two pans if needed.
- High heat creates caramelization.

Source: Serious Eats (Kenji López-Alt tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/roasted-vegetables/

---

# Salmon Patties

Old-fashioned comfort, crispy outside

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 (14.75 oz) can salmon, drained
- ¼ cup onion, minced
- ¼ cup cornmeal
- 1 egg, beaten
- 3 tbsp flour
- Vegetable oil for frying
- Lemon and tartar sauce

## Steps

1. Drain salmon, flake into bowl, remove bones and skin.
2. Add onion, cornmeal, egg, flour. Mix well.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Form into 4-6 patties.
5. Heat ¼ inch oil in skillet over medium.
6. Fry until golden, 3-4 minutes per side.
7. Drain. Serve with lemon and tartar sauce.

## Notes

- Canned salmon works perfectly.
- Cornmeal gives authentic Southern texture.

Source: AllRecipes (2,000+ reviews)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/salmon-patties/

---

# Salmon Rice Bowl

The viral salmon bowl, seared fresh instead of microwaved.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 15 min · Serves: 2

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The sauce carries it - make double and put it on everything.

_Tested 2026-07. Dropped the false 'crispy rice' claim (nothing crisped the rice), added a salmon pull temp, and credited Mariko's actual method._

## Ingredients

- 2 salmon fillets (6 oz each), skin-on
- 2 cups cooked sushi rice, warm
- 1/2 cup kimchi, chopped
- 1 avocado, sliced
- 2 sheets nori, cut into strips
- 2 green onions, sliced
- Sesame seeds
- 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
- Diamond Crystal kosher salt and black pepper
**For the sauce:**
- 3 Tbsp Kewpie mayo
- 1 Tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tsp sriracha
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 1/2 tsp sugar

## Steps

1. Mix all sauce ingredients in a small bowl. Set aside.
2. Pat salmon dry with paper towels. Season skin side with salt. Season flesh side with salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side down. Press gently with a spatula for the first 30 seconds to prevent curling.
4. Cook 4-5 minutes until skin is deeply crispy and releases easily. Flip and cook 2-3 more minutes - pull at 125°F internal for medium (or 145°F if you want USDA-done).
5. Divide rice between two bowls. Top with kimchi, avocado slices, and nori strips.
6. Place salmon on top, skin-side up so it stays crunchy. Drizzle generously with sauce.
7. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

## Notes

- The crispy skin is the point. Bone-dry salmon, hot pan, don't rush the skin side.
- Kewpie mayo is richer than regular mayo (extra yolk, MSG) - Asian grocery or the international aisle.
- Add a jammy egg (6½-min boil) for extra protein.
- The original: Mariko flakes leftover salmon into rice, microwaves it with an ice cube on top for steam, then adds the same sauce. Works great with leftovers - this recipe is for when you're starting fresh.

## When it goes wrong

- Skin stuck to the pan → fish was wet or pan wasn't hot; dry hard, heat until the oil shimmers, and don't move it early.
- Skin crisp then turned soggy → sat skin-side down in the bowl; always plate skin-up.
- Sauce tastes flat → regular mayo missing the Kewpie richness; add a pinch of sugar and a drop of sesame oil.

Source: Inspired by Emily Mariko / TikTok
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/salmon-rice-bowl/

---

# Fresh Salsa

Bright, chunky, restaurant style

Makes: 2 cups

## Ingredients

- 1 lb ripe tomatoes, diced
- ¼ cup white onion, minced
- 1-2 jalapeños, seeded and minced
- ¼ cup cilantro, chopped
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- Juice of 1 lime
- Salt to taste

## Steps

1. Combine tomatoes, onion, jalapeño, cilantro, garlic in bowl.
2. Add lime juice and salt. Toss gently.
3. Let sit 15 minutes for flavors to meld.
4. Taste and adjust seasoning.
5. Best served within a few hours.

## Notes

- Ripe tomatoes make all the difference.
- Seed jalapeños for less heat.
- Salt draws out tomato juices.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/salsa/

---

# Scrambled Eggs

Gordon Ramsay's method. Creamy, silky, life-changing.

Prep: 1 min · Cook: 4 min · Serves: 2

## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3 tbsp butter, cubed
- 1 tbsp crème fraîche (or sour cream)
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh chives, chopped (optional)

## Steps

1. Crack eggs directly into a COLD nonstick saucepan. Add butter. Don't whisk yet.
2. Place pan over medium heat. Start stirring continuously with a rubber spatula, breaking up the yolks and combining with whites.
3. As eggs start to thicken, pull pan OFF the heat. Keep stirring for 20 seconds.
4. Return to heat. Stir constantly. When eggs start to form curds again, pull OFF heat again. Keep stirring.
5. Repeat this on/off process 3-4 times over about 3 minutes. The eggs should be soft and slightly underdone.
6. When almost done but still slightly wet, remove from heat completely. Stir in crème fraîche—this stops the cooking.
7. Season with salt and pepper NOW (not before—early salt makes eggs watery).
8. Serve immediately topped with chives. Don't let them sit.

## Notes

- COLD pan start: This lets eggs cook slowly and evenly from the beginning.
- ON/OFF the heat: This prevents overcooking. The pan stays hot even off the burner.
- Remove while still wet: Carryover heat finishes them. If they look done in the pan, they're overdone on the plate.
- Season LAST: Salt draws moisture from eggs. Season after cooking for best texture.
- Crème fraîche: Adds creaminess AND stops cooking. Sour cream works too.

Source: Gordon Ramsay's method
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/scrambled-eggs/

---

# Shakshuka

Eggs poached in spiced tomato sauce. One skillet, all the flavor.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 Tbsp olive oil
- 1 medium onion, diced
- 1 red bell pepper, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp cayenne (optional)
- 1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatoes
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 6 large eggs
- 1/4 cup feta cheese, crumbled
- Fresh cilantro or parsley for serving
- Crusty bread for serving

## Steps

1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper. Sauté 5-7 minutes until softened.
2. Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and cayenne. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Pour in crushed tomatoes, salt, and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
4. Make 6 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well.
5. Cover and cook 5-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
6. Top with feta and fresh herbs. Serve immediately from the skillet with crusty bread.

## Notes

- Yolk doneness: 5-6 minutes for runny, 8-10 for set. Cover traps steam and cooks tops of eggs.
- Spice level: Cayenne is optional. Harissa paste is a more authentic addition.
- Add-ins: Chorizo, merguez, or crispy chickpeas for protein.
- Feta: Not traditional but common. Goat cheese works too.
- The bread: Non-negotiable. You need something to soak up the sauce.

Source: Adapted from NYT Cooking
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/shakshuka/

---

# Shepherd's Pie

Savory meat filling topped with mashed potatoes

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs russet potatoes, cubed
- 6 tbsp butter, divided
- ½ cup heavy cream
- 1½ lbs ground beef
- 1 onion, diced
- 2 carrots, diced
- 2 celery ribs, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 cup beef broth
- 1 cup frozen peas
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire
- 1 tsp thyme

## Steps

1. Boil potatoes until tender. Mash with 4 tbsp butter and cream. Season.
2. Brown beef, breaking into pieces. Drain, transfer out.
3. Cook vegetables in remaining butter until soft, 8 minutes.
4. Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 1 minute.
5. Return beef. Add broth, peas, Worcestershire, thyme. Simmer 10 minutes. Season.
6. Spread mashed potatoes over filling. Rough up surface with fork.
7. Broil until golden, 5-10 minutes.

## Notes

- 'Shepherd's pie' uses lamb; 'cottage pie' uses beef.
- Roughing up potatoes creates crispy peaks.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/shepherds-pie/

---

# Shrimp Boil

One-pot feast, dump on newspaper

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 3 lbs shell-on large shrimp
- 2 lbs small red potatoes
- 4 ears corn, halved
- 1 lb andouille sausage, sliced
- ½ cup Old Bay
- 4 cloves garlic
- 2 lemons, halved
- 1 stick butter, melted
- Hot sauce

## Steps

1. Fill large pot with 4 gallons water. Add Old Bay, garlic, lemons, 2 tbsp salt. Boil.
2. Add potatoes. Cook 10 minutes.
3. Add sausage and corn. Cook 8 minutes.
4. Add shrimp. Cook 3 minutes until just pink.
5. Drain immediately.
6. Dump onto newspaper-covered table.
7. Serve with melted butter and hot sauce.

## Notes

- Add items in order of cook time.
- Drain immediately when shrimp turn pink.
- Old Bay is non-negotiable.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/shrimp-boil/

---

# Shrimp Scampi

Garlicky, buttery, perfectly cooked shrimp

Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs shell-on jumbo shrimp
- 3 tbsp salt + 2 tbsp sugar (for brine)
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 cup dry white wine
- 4 sprigs fresh thyme
- 3 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp cornstarch
- 8 cloves garlic, sliced thin
- ½ tsp red pepper flakes
- 4 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp parsley

## Steps

1. Peel shrimp, reserve shells. Brine shrimp in salt, sugar, 1 quart water for 15 minutes.
2. Cook shells in 1 tbsp oil until spotty brown. Add wine and thyme, simmer 5 minutes. Strain, reserve liquid.
3. Mix lemon juice and cornstarch.
4. Cook garlic and pepper flakes in oil over medium-low until golden, 3-5 minutes.
5. Add wine mixture, simmer. Add shrimp, cover, cook 5-7 minutes.
6. Transfer shrimp. Add lemon-cornstarch mixture, cook until thickened.
7. Off heat, whisk in butter and parsley. Return shrimp, toss.

## Notes

- Brining keeps shrimp juicy.
- Making stock from shells adds depth.
- Poaching in wine cooks shrimp more evenly.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK's #1 most popular recipe)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/shrimp-scampi/

---

# Sloppy Joes

Sweet, tangy, messy perfection

Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs ground beef
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 onion, diced fine
- 1 green bell pepper, diced fine
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup ketchup
- 2 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire
- 1 tbsp yellow mustard
- 1 tsp chili powder
- ½ cup water
- Hamburger buns

## Steps

1. Cook onion and pepper in oil until soft, 5 minutes.
2. Add beef, break into small pieces. Brown 8 minutes. Drain fat.
3. Add garlic, 30 seconds.
4. Stir in ketchup, sugar, Worcestershire, mustard, chili powder, water.
5. Simmer until thick, 10-15 minutes. Season to taste.
6. Toast buns. Pile on the sloppy joe.

## Notes

- Break meat small for classic texture.
- Let simmer until thick.
- Vinegar at end adds brightness.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/sloppy-joes/

---

# Smash Burgers

Crispy lacy edges, diner-style perfection. Fast and simple.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 10 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 1 lb ground beef chuck (80/20)
- 8 slices American cheese
- 4 burger buns (potato buns work great)
- Kosher salt
- Black pepper
- 2 tbsp butter for buns
- Suggested Toppings: Pickle slices, thinly sliced onion, ketchup, mustard, mayo, shredded lettuce

## Steps

1. Divide beef into 8 equal balls (2 oz each). Don't pack them tight—keep them loose.
2. Heat cast iron skillet or griddle over HIGH heat until smoking hot. This is crucial.
3. Place 2-4 balls on the hot surface (don't crowd). Immediately smash flat with a sturdy spatula. Press HARD for 10 seconds. Should be very thin.
4. Season the top with salt and pepper AFTER smashing (not before).
5. Cook 2 minutes until edges are brown and crispy. Flip.
6. Immediately add cheese slice. Cook 1 more minute.
7. Stack 2 patties per burger.
8. Toast buns in the beef fat on the same pan. Assemble and serve immediately.

## Notes

- Why season AFTER smashing: Salt draws moisture from raw meat. Salting after = better crust.
- American cheese: Melts better than anything else. This is not the time for fancy cheese.
- The smash: Press as thin as possible. The goal is maximum surface contact = maximum crust.
- Why 2 thin patties: More crust per burger. That crust is the whole point.
- Ventilation: This creates smoke. Open windows or turn on the fan.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats / Kenji López-Alt
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/smash-burgers/

---

# Snickerdoodles

Soft, chewy, and rolled in cinnamon sugar. The cookie that hugs you back.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 10 min · Makes: 24 cookies

## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup (1 stick) butter, softened
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 large egg
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp cream of tartar
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 1/4 tsp salt
**For rolling:**
- 3 Tbsp sugar
- 1 Tbsp cinnamon

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Line baking sheets with parchment.
2. Beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes.
3. Add egg and vanilla. Beat until combined.
4. In another bowl, whisk flour, cream of tartar, baking soda, and salt.
5. Add dry ingredients to wet. Mix until just combined.
6. Mix cinnamon and sugar in a small bowl.
7. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Roll each ball in cinnamon sugar.
8. Place 2 inches apart on baking sheets. Bake 8-10 minutes until edges are set but centers are soft.
9. Cool on pan 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

## Notes

- Cream of tartar: Don't skip it—it gives snickerdoodles their signature tangy flavor and chewy texture.
- Soft centers: Pull them when edges are just set. They firm up as they cool.
- Crinkle tops: The cookies should crack and crinkle. That's the sign of a good snickerdoodle.
- Chill the dough: For thicker cookies, refrigerate dough 30 minutes before rolling.
- Storage: Keep in an airtight container up to 1 week.

Source: Adapted from Sally's Baking Addiction
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/snickerdoodles/

---

# Spaghetti & Meatballs

Tender meatballs, quick marinara. Weeknight-friendly.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 45 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- Meatballs: 1 lb ground beef (80/20), ½ lb ground pork, ½ cup panko breadcrumbs, ¼ cup milk, 1 egg, ¼ cup parmesan grated, 3 garlic cloves minced, 2 tbsp fresh parsley chopped, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper
- Sauce & Pasta: 28 oz crushed tomatoes, 3 garlic cloves minced, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp Italian seasoning, 1 lb spaghetti, fresh basil for serving

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Line baking sheet with parchment.
2. Make the panade: Mix panko and milk in large bowl. Let sit 5 minutes until absorbed.
3. Add beef, pork, egg, parmesan, garlic, parsley, salt, and pepper to bowl. Mix gently with hands until just combined. Don't overwork.
4. Roll into golf ball-sized meatballs (about 20). Place on baking sheet. Bake 15-20 minutes until browned.
5. While meatballs bake, make sauce: Heat oil in large pan over medium. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and Italian seasoning. Simmer 10 minutes.
6. Add baked meatballs to sauce. Simmer together 15-20 minutes.
7. Cook spaghetti according to package. Drain, reserving 1 cup pasta water.
8. Toss pasta with sauce, adding pasta water if needed. Serve meatballs on top with fresh basil.

## Notes

- The panade secret: Soaking breadcrumbs in milk keeps meatballs tender and moist. Don't skip this.
- Why oven-bake? Less messy than pan-frying, cooks evenly, still gets nice browning.
- All beef works: Use 1½ lbs ground beef if you don't have pork.
- Storage: Meatballs keep 4 days refrigerated, freeze beautifully for 3 months.

Source: Adapted from Once Upon a Chef
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/spaghetti-and-meatballs/

---

# Split Pea Soup

Thick, smoky, and deeply satisfying

Serves: 6-8

## Ingredients

- 1 smoked ham hock
- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 celery ribs, chopped
- 2 carrots, chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 lb green split peas, rinsed
- 8 cups water
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp fresh thyme
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Melt butter. Cook onions, celery, carrots until softened, 8-10 minutes.
2. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
3. Add peas, water, ham hock, bay leaves, thyme. Bring to boil.
4. Reduce heat, simmer partially covered until thick, about 1 hour.
5. Remove ham hock, shred meat, return to soup. Discard bone.
6. Remove bay leaves. Season with salt and pepper.

## Notes

- No need to soak split peas.
- Soup thickens significantly as it cools - thin when reheating.

Source: America's Test Kitchen (ATK Lab-Tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/split-pea-soup/

---

# Stuffed Shells

Ricotta-filled pasta in marinara

Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 (12 oz) box jumbo shells
- 1 lb ground beef
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 (24 oz) jar marinara
- 32 oz ricotta
- 2 cups mozzarella, shredded
- ½ cup Parmesan
- 2 eggs
- ¼ cup parsley
- 1 tsp Italian seasoning

## Steps

1. Cook shells until just al dente. Cool.
2. Brown beef with onion. Stir in half the marinara.
3. Mix ricotta, 1 cup mozzarella, Parmesan, eggs, parsley, Italian seasoning.
4. Spread remaining marinara in 9x13 dish.
5. Fill shells with ricotta, place in dish. Top with meat sauce and remaining mozzarella.
6. Cover, bake at 350°F for 40 minutes. Uncover, bake 10 more minutes.

## Notes

- Undercook shells so they don't tear.
- Piping bag makes filling faster.

Source: AllRecipes (2,700+ five-star reviews)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/stuffed-shells/

---

# Stuffing

Herbed bread, savory, buttery

Serves: 10-12

## Ingredients

- 1 lb crusty bread, cubed and dried
- 6 tbsp butter
- 1 large onion, diced
- 3 celery ribs, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh sage, chopped
- 1 tbsp fresh thyme
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 2 eggs, beaten
- Salt and pepper

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter 9x13 dish.
2. Dry bread cubes in oven 15 minutes if not already stale.
3. Melt butter. Cook onion and celery until soft, 8 minutes.
4. Add garlic, sage, thyme. Cook 1 minute. Season.
5. Combine bread, vegetables, broth, eggs. Toss well.
6. Transfer to dish. Cover with foil.
7. Bake 30 minutes covered. Uncover, bake 20 more minutes until golden.

## Notes

- Dried bread absorbs more liquid.
- Fresh herbs make a huge difference.

Source: Serious Eats (Rigorously tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/stuffing/

---

# Sugar Cookies

Crisp edges, soft center, perfect for decorating

Makes: 24-36

## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter softened
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tsp vanilla
- ½ tsp almond extract (optional)
- 3 cups flour
- 1½ tsp baking powder
- ½ tsp salt
- Royal icing for decorating

## Steps

1. Beat butter and sugar until fluffy, 3 minutes.
2. Add egg and extracts.
3. Whisk flour, baking powder, salt. Add gradually.
4. Divide dough, flatten, wrap, chill 2 hours.
5. Roll to ¼-inch thick. Cut shapes.
6. Place on parchment sheets. Chill 15 minutes.
7. Bake at 350°F for 8-10 minutes until edges just start to color.
8. Cool completely before decorating.

## Notes

- Chilling prevents spreading.
- Don't overbake - they firm as they cool.
- Almond extract is the secret.

Source: King Arthur Baking (King Arthur tested)
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/sugar-cookies/

---

# Swedish Meatballs

Tender meatballs in creamy gravy. Better than IKEA.

Prep: 25 min · Cook: 25 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 1 lb ground beef
- ½ lb ground pork
- 1 egg
- ¼ cup breadcrumbs
- ¼ cup milk
- 1 tsp salt
- ¼ tsp allspice
- ¼ tsp nutmeg
- 3 tbsp butter
- 3 tbsp flour
- 2 cups beef broth
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce

## Steps

1. Soak breadcrumbs in milk for 5 minutes.
2. Mix beef, pork, egg, soaked breadcrumbs, salt, allspice, and nutmeg. Don't overmix.
3. Roll into 1-inch balls. You should get about 30.
4. Brown meatballs in batches in butter over medium heat. Set aside.
5. In same pan, whisk flour into drippings. Cook 1 minute.
6. Slowly whisk in beef broth, then cream. Simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes.
7. Add Worcestershire. Return meatballs to sauce.
8. Simmer 10 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
9. Serve over egg noodles or mashed potatoes.

## Notes

- Allspice is key: It's the signature Swedish flavor.
- Don't skip the pork: It keeps meatballs tender.
- Make ahead: Meatballs freeze well in sauce.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/swedish-meatballs/

---

# Sweet Potato Casserole

Is it a side dish or dessert? Yes. The Thanksgiving essential.

Prep: 20 min · Cook: 45 min · Serves: 10

## Ingredients

**For the filling:**
- 3 lbs sweet potatoes (about 4 large)
- 1/2 cup butter, melted
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/2 tsp cinnamon
- 1/4 tsp nutmeg
- 1/2 tsp salt
**For the topping:**
- 1 cup pecans, chopped
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1/3 cup flour
- 3 Tbsp butter, cold and cubed

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
2. Bake or boil sweet potatoes until tender, about 45-60 minutes for baking or 20-25 minutes for boiling. Peel and mash.
3. Mix mashed sweet potatoes with melted butter, brown sugar, eggs, vanilla, milk, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt until smooth.
4. Spread filling in prepared baking dish.
5. Make topping: Mix pecans, brown sugar, and flour. Cut in cold butter until crumbly.
6. Sprinkle topping evenly over sweet potato filling.
7. Bake 30 minutes until topping is golden and filling is set.

## Notes

- Sweet potatoes vs yams: Use orange-fleshed sweet potatoes. They're often mislabeled as yams in stores.
- Canned shortcut: Substitute two 29 oz cans of sweet potato purée for fresh.
- Marshmallow version: Skip the streusel topping. Top with mini marshmallows and broil 1-2 minutes until golden.
- Make ahead: Prepare filling a day ahead and refrigerate. Add topping just before baking.
- Smaller batch: Halve the recipe for a smaller gathering—use an 8x8 pan.

Source: Adapted from Once Upon a Chef
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/sweet-potato-casserole/

---

# Teriyaki Chicken

Homemade teriyaki sauce beats bottled every time. Sweet, salty, and glossy.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 20 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs
- 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
- 1/2 cup soy sauce
- 1/4 cup mirin (or 3 Tbsp rice vinegar + 1 Tbsp sugar)
- 1/4 cup sake (or dry sherry)
- 3 Tbsp brown sugar
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp fresh ginger, grated
- 1 Tbsp cornstarch + 2 Tbsp water
- Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish

## Steps

1. Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sake, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger in a bowl. Set aside.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and cook 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Remove to a plate.
3. Pour teriyaki sauce into the skillet. Bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits.
4. Mix cornstarch with water. Stir into the sauce and cook 1-2 minutes until thickened.
5. Return chicken to the skillet. Coat in sauce and cook 1-2 minutes, flipping to glaze both sides.
6. Slice chicken and serve over rice. Drizzle with extra sauce, sesame seeds, and green onions.

## Notes

- Real teriyaki: The word means 'glossy grilled.' The sauce should be sticky and shiny.
- Mirin: This sweet rice wine is key. Find it in the Asian aisle. Don't skip it.
- No sake: Dry sherry or white wine work. Or use more mirin.
- Grilled version: Grill chicken and brush with sauce during the last few minutes.
- Chicken breast: Works but thighs are juicier and more forgiving.

Source: Adapted from Just One Cookbook
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/teriyaki-chicken/

---

# Tomato Soup

Velvety smooth, deeply tomatoey. Grilled cheese's best friend.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 30 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp butter
- 1 onion, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 cans 28oz whole peeled tomatoes
- 2 cups chicken or vegetable broth
- 1 tsp sugar
- ½ cup heavy cream
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Fresh basil for garnish

## Steps

1. Melt butter in large pot over medium heat.
2. Sauté onion until soft, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
3. Add tomatoes with juices, broth, and sugar. Bring to simmer.
4. Cook 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Blend until smooth with immersion blender (or in batches in regular blender).
6. Stir in cream. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve hot, garnished with basil.

## Notes

- San Marzano tomatoes: Worth the splurge for best flavor.
- Don't boil after adding cream: It can curdle.
- Make it chunky: Blend only half for more texture.

Source: Adapted from America's Test Kitchen
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/tomato-soup/

---

# Tuna Noodle Casserole

Creamy, comforting, budget-friendly classic.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 30 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 12 oz egg noodles
- 4 tbsp butter
- 1 onion, diced
- 2 stalks celery, diced
- ¼ cup flour
- 2 cups milk
- 1 cup chicken broth
- 2 cans 5oz tuna, drained
- 1 cup frozen peas
- 1 cup shredded cheddar
- ½ cup breadcrumbs
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Cook noodles according to package, drain.
2. Melt butter in large oven-safe skillet. Sauté onion and celery until soft.
3. Whisk in flour, cook 1 minute.
4. Gradually whisk in milk and broth. Simmer until thickened.
5. Remove from heat. Stir in tuna, peas, half the cheese, noodles. Season.
6. Top with remaining cheese and breadcrumbs.
7. Bake 20-25 minutes until bubbly and golden.

## Notes

- Upgrade the tuna: Oil-packed has better flavor than water-packed.
- Add mushrooms: Sauté with the onion for extra depth.
- Crispy top: Broil last 2 minutes for extra crunch.

Source: Adapted from AllRecipes
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/tuna-noodle-casserole/

---

# Tuna Salad

Classic lunch, perfectly balanced. Not too dry, not too wet.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 4

## Ingredients

- 2 cans 5oz tuna, drained
- ¼ cup mayonnaise
- 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
- 2 stalks celery, finely diced
- 2 tbsp red onion, minced
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Optional: dill, capers, relish

## Steps

1. Drain tuna well and flake into a bowl.
2. Add mayo, mustard, celery, onion, and lemon juice.
3. Mix gently—don't mash the tuna to paste.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Serve on bread, crackers, or over greens.

## Notes

- Good tuna matters: Try olive oil-packed for richer flavor.
- Drain well: Wet tuna = watery salad.
- Variations: Add chopped pickle, fresh dill, or a pinch of curry powder.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/tuna-salad/

---

# Basic Vinaigrette

Master this ratio and never buy bottled dressing again.

Prep: 5 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 3 tbsp red wine vinegar
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- ½ cup extra virgin olive oil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Optional: honey, shallot, herbs

## Steps

1. Whisk vinegar, mustard, garlic, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
2. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking constantly.
3. Taste and adjust seasoning.
4. Use immediately or store refrigerated up to 1 week.

## Notes

- The ratio: 3 parts oil to 1 part acid. Adjust to taste.
- Mustard is the emulsifier: It helps oil and vinegar stay mixed.
- Variations: Swap vinegar for lemon juice. Add honey for sweetness. Use shallot instead of garlic.

Source: Adapted from Serious Eats
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/vinaigrette/

---

# Crispy Waffles

Crispy outside, fluffy inside. The cornstarch is the secret.

Prep: 10 min · Cook: 20 min · Serves: 6

## Ingredients

- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- ¼ cup cornstarch
- 1 tbsp baking powder
- ½ tsp salt
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 2 eggs, separated
- 2 cups milk
- ½ cup vegetable oil
- 1 tsp vanilla

## Steps

1. Preheat waffle iron.
2. Whisk flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
3. In another bowl, whisk egg yolks, milk, oil, and vanilla.
4. Beat egg whites to stiff peaks.
5. Pour wet into dry, stir until just combined.
6. Fold in egg whites gently—lumps are okay.
7. Cook according to your waffle iron instructions.
8. Serve immediately for maximum crispness.

## Notes

- Cornstarch = crispy: It's the secret to that crunch.
- Whipped whites = fluffy: Don't skip this step.
- Don't stack: Keep waffles on a wire rack in warm oven to stay crispy.

Source: Adapted from King Arthur Baking
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/waffles/

---

# Watermelon Feta Salad

Cold, salty, sweet. July in a bowl.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 6

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The doubters take one bite and go quiet - the oldest trick in the summer playbook for a reason.

_Tested 2026-07. Draining the melon and soaking the onion turn this from soggy-and-harsh to the right version._

## Ingredients

- 6 cups (about 900g) seedless watermelon, in 1-inch cubes, cold
- 150g (5 oz) feta in block form, broken into rough chunks
- 1/2 English cucumber, halved and sliced (optional, adds crunch)
- 1/4 small red onion, shaved thin and soaked 10 min in cold water
- 1/2 cup fresh mint leaves, torn
- Zest and juice of 1 lime
- 3 Tbsp extra virgin olive oil
- Flaky sea salt and black pepper

## Steps

1. Cube the watermelon and drain it in a colander 10 minutes - a watery salad is the classic failure.
2. Whisk lime zest, lime juice, and olive oil.
3. Arrange watermelon, cucumber, and drained onion on a platter or in a wide shallow bowl.
4. Scatter the feta chunks over the top. Rough hand-broken chunks beat neat crumbles - you want creamy pockets.
5. Drizzle the lime oil over everything, then finish with torn mint, flaky salt, and a few grinds of pepper.
6. Serve immediately, cold. This salad has a 30-minute lifespan.

## Notes

- Buy feta in a block in brine - pre-crumbled is dry and coated in anti-caking starch.
- Assemble at the last minute; salt pulls water out of watermelon fast.
- Add a handful of Kalamata olives if you want it more Greek, a drizzle of hot honey if you want it 2026.

## When it goes wrong

- Pink puddle → melon not drained or salad sat too long; drain, and serve within half an hour.
- Aggressive onion breath → skipped the cold-water soak; ten minutes tames it.
- Feta disappeared into mush → tossed instead of scattered; feta goes on top, last.
- Tastes flat → underripe melon; if it isn't sweet on its own, no dressing saves it.

Source: rcpbx original
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/watermelon-feta-salad/

---

# White Chicken Chili

Creamy, warming, and lighter than red chili.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 30 min · Serves: 8

## Ingredients

- 1 lb chicken breast
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 onion, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 cans 15oz white beans, drained
- 4 cups chicken broth
- 2 cans 4oz green chiles
- 1 tsp cumin
- ½ tsp oregano
- ¼ tsp cayenne
- 4 oz cream cheese
- Sour cream, cilantro, lime for serving

## Steps

1. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Sear in oil until cooked through. Remove and shred.
2. Sauté onion until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
3. Add beans, broth, chiles, cumin, oregano, cayenne. Bring to simmer.
4. Cook 15 minutes. Mash some beans against the pot to thicken.
5. Stir in cream cheese until melted. Add shredded chicken.
6. Simmer 5 more minutes.
7. Serve topped with sour cream, cilantro, and lime.

## Notes

- Rotisserie chicken: Great shortcut—use 3 cups shredded.
- More heat: Add diced jalapeño with the onion.
- Even better next day: Flavors meld overnight.

Source: Adapted from AllRecipes
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/white-chicken-chili/

---

# Yogurt Tiramisu

The viral Biscoff-yogurt tiramisu. Zero mascarpone, zero regret.

Prep: 15 min (plus overnight chill) · Cook: 0 min · Serves: 6

**Verdict: WORTH IT** — The rare viral dessert that's better than its video - and it's four ingredients.

_Tested 2026-07. Two-second dunks and an overnight chill are the whole game - longer dunks collapse, shorter chills taste like a parfait._

## Ingredients

- 2 cups (450g) full-fat Greek yogurt (5% - this is not the place for 0%)
- 3 Tbsp maple syrup or honey
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- Pinch of fine salt
- 3/4 cup (180ml) strong brewed coffee or espresso, cooled
- 1 tsp cocoa powder plus more for dusting
- 28-32 Biscoff cookies (about 1 1/2 packs) or 18 ladyfingers
- Dark chocolate, for grating (optional)

## Steps

1. Stir yogurt, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt until smooth. Taste - it should be just sweet enough, not dessert-sweet; the cookies bring plenty.
2. Pour the cooled coffee into a shallow bowl.
3. Dunk cookies one at a time, 1-2 seconds per side - in and out. Biscoff dissolves fast.
4. Lay a layer of dunked cookies in an 8x8 dish. Spread half the yogurt over them.
5. Repeat: dunked cookies, remaining yogurt. Smooth the top.
6. Cover and refrigerate at least 6 hours, ideally overnight. The cookies soften into cake and the layers set.
7. Dust generously with cocoa (and grated chocolate if using) just before serving.

## Notes

- Overnight is the recipe. At hour two it's cookies and yogurt; at hour twelve it's tiramisu.
- Full-fat yogurt reads as cream. Low-fat reads as breakfast.
- Real-deal upgrade: fold 1/2 cup whipped cream into the yogurt for a lighter, richer layer.

## When it goes wrong

- Soggy sludge layer → cookies dunked too long; two seconds means two seconds.
- Tastes like plain yogurt → underchilled or unsalted; overnight, and don't skip the pinch of salt.
- Runny layers → thin yogurt; use thick Greek 5%, or strain regular yogurt an hour in a coffee filter.
- Bitter top → cocoa dusted too early absorbs moisture and clumps; dust at serving time.

Source: TikTok viral, spring-summer 2026
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/yogurt-tiramisu/

---

# Zucchini Bread

Moist, lightly spiced, and the best way to use summer's zucchini overflow.

Prep: 15 min · Cook: 55 min · Makes: 1 loaf (10 slices)

## Ingredients

- 2 cups shredded zucchini (about 2 medium)
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1/3 cup vegetable oil
- 2 large eggs
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1/2 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 1/2 tsp cinnamon
- 1/4 tsp nutmeg
- 1/2 cup walnuts, chopped (optional)
- 1/2 cup chocolate chips (optional)

## Steps

1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x5 inch loaf pan.
2. Shred zucchini and squeeze out excess moisture with a clean kitchen towel. You should have about 1 1/2 cups after squeezing.
3. In a large bowl, whisk sugar, oil, eggs, and vanilla until combined.
4. In another bowl, whisk flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
5. Add dry ingredients to wet ingredients and stir until just combined. Fold in zucchini and walnuts or chocolate chips if using.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake 50-60 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
7. Cool in pan 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely.

## Notes

- Squeeze the zucchini: This is the most important step. Excess moisture = soggy bread.
- Don't overmix: Stir until just combined. Overmixing makes the bread tough.
- Storage: Wrap tightly and store at room temperature 3-4 days, or freeze up to 3 months.
- Double it: This recipe doubles perfectly for two loaves.
- Muffins: Pour into lined muffin tins and bake 20-25 minutes.

Source: Adapted from Once Upon a Chef
Canonical: https://rcpbx.com/recipes/zucchini-bread/

---
